Where Better Hearing Begins

Whether you’re experiencing hearing loss, need hearing aid care, or want to protect your hearing for the future, we’re here to help.

Book Appointment
Call Us NOw

Granger, IN Audiologists & Hearing Aids

Campbell Hearing Solutions

Campbell Hearing Solutions provides expert hearing care to help you stay connected to the sounds that matter most. From comprehensive hearing evaluations to advanced hearing aid solutions, our Granger, IN audiologists are here to support your hearing health with personalized care. Schedule an appointment today and take the first step toward better hearing!

We offer a full range of hearing services to help you hear clearly and comfortably. Our expert audiologists provide comprehensive hearing assessments to diagnose hearing loss and recommend personalized solutions. Whether you need a hearing test, tinnitus treatment, or expert guidance on hearing aids, we’re here to support your hearing health every step of the way.

Book Appointment
Call Us NOw

what we do

Audiology Services in Granger, IN

Hearing Evaluations

Better hearing starts with a comprehensive evaluation. Our audiologists use advanced testing tools to identify hearing loss and recommend personalized solutions that suit your lifestyle.

learn more
Hearing Aid Fittings

Comfort and clarity go hand-in-hand. We tailor each hearing aid to fit your ears and your unique hearing needs, ensuring a natural, high-quality listening experience.

learn more
Hearing Aid Services

From choosing the right hearing aids to keeping them in top condition, we’re with you every step of the way. Services include fittings, programming, cleaning, and repairs—so your hearing aids always perform their best.

learn more
Tinnitus Evaluations & Support

If you hear ringing, buzzing, musical, or hissing sounds, our tinnitus evaluations can help uncover the cause. We provide proven treatment options—like sound therapy and management strategies—for long-term relief.

learn more
Diagnostic Services

We offer detailed diagnostic testing to assess your hearing health and detect any underlying issues. Our experienced team delivers accurate results and guides you toward the most effective treatment plan.

learn more
Our Solutions

Hearing Aids & Protection in Granger, IN

Hearing Aids

Explore our full selection of modern, discreet hearing aids designed to fit your lifestyle. Our experts will help you choose the best device for natural, clear sound.

learn more
Hearing Aid Batteries and Accessories

Keep your hearing aids powered and performing with our high-quality batteries, chargers, and accessories—everything you need in one convenient place.

learn more
Custom Ear Protection

Protect your hearing with custom-fit earplugs and earmolds—ideal for noise, swimming, musicians, and more. Designed for comfort, durability, and maximum protection.

learn more

Supported Hearing Aid Brands

Testimonials

What Our Patients Are Saying

Your hearing health is our top priority, and we are dedicated to providing compassionate care. Hear from our patients in Granger, IN, about their experiences with our services!

Frequently Asked Questions

Can’t find the answers you’re looking for? Reach out us.

contact us
How do I know if I have hearing loss?

Hearing loss can develop slowly, making it hard to notice at first. Common signs include:

  • Frequently asking people to repeat themselves
  • Struggling to hear conversations in noisy places
  • Turning up the volume on the TV or phone higher than others prefer
  • Feeling like others are mumbling or speaking too softly
  • Difficulty understanding high-pitched voices, such as children or women
  • Ringing or buzzing in the ears (tinnitus)
  • Feeling exhausted from trying to listen

If any of these sound familiar, it’s a good idea to get a hearing test. An audiologist can check your hearing and help you find the right solution. Catching hearing loss early can make a big difference in your daily life and communication.

Can you self-refer to an audiologist?

Yes! You can make an appointment with an audiologist on your own—no referral from a doctor or nurse is needed. This is called self-referral and makes it easy to get the hearing care you need. Just keep in mind that some insurance plans, including Medicare, may require a referral for coverage.

How is hearing loss diagnosed?

Hearing loss is diagnosed through a complete hearing evaluation done by an audiologist or hearing instrument specialist. This includes a series of tests to measure how well you hear and check for any related issues. After the testing, the audiologist will review your results, explain the type and degree of hearing loss (if any), and recommend next steps, like hearing aids, medical care, or other solutions.

What type of hearing aid do I need?

The right hearing aid depends on your hearing needs, lifestyle, and preferences. Hearing aids come in different styles and offer various features. Your audiologist will help you choose the best option based on your hearing level, how active you are, how comfortable you are with small devices, and what look you prefer.

bodyHtml:
Skip to content

Home › About

About Campbell Hearing Solutions

Campbell Hearing Solutions is an audiology and hearing aid clinic with expert audiologists providing hearing tests, tinnitus evaluations, and professional hearing services in Granger, IN.

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, we’re dedicated to helping you reconnect with the sounds you love. Located in the heart of Granger, IN, our practice offers compassionate, personalized care for individuals experiencing hearing loss or seeking better hearing support.

Our clinic is built on trust, honesty, and a commitment to patient-centered care. We take the time to listen, learn about your lifestyle, and create hearing solutions tailored specifically for you.

From comprehensive hearing evaluations to advanced hearing aid fittings and follow-up care, we’re here to walk with you through every step of your hearing journey. We work with leading hearing aid brands to offer the latest technology that’s comfortable, discreet, and effective.

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, you’re not just a patient—you’re a valued part of our community. Whether you’re exploring hearing aids for the first time or looking for a second opinion, we’re here to help with expert guidance and warm, friendly service.

Let us help you hear your world more clearly. Visit us in Granger and experience the difference personalized hearing care can make.

Our Team

Sheree Richardson, M.S. CCC-A

Audiologist

Sheree Richardson is an audiologist providing hearing tests and hearing aids in Granger, IN. Sheree is a northwest Indiana native and a 1985 graduate of Purdue University. She brings a strong clinical background and extensive knowledge of hearing aids to her work, with years of experience in otolaryngology clinics, hospital...

READ MORE

Karen Smith

Audiology Assistant

Karen has been with Campbell Hearing Solutions for over 20 years. She has over 25 years of experience working in front and back medical offices, including audiology and ENT.

...
READ MORE

Campbell Hearing Solutions

Phone

(574) 999‑2853

Address

209 Florence Ave. Granger, IN 46530

Email Us

Use our online contact form to email us.

Clinic Hours

Mon - Thurs: 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM
Friday: 8:00 AM - 2:00 PM

Call Us Now

bodyHtml:
Skip to content

Home › Campbell Hearing Solutions Audiology Services

Campbell Hearing Solutions Audiology Services

Campbell Hearing Solutions provides expert hearing care to help you stay connected to the sounds that matter most. From comprehensive hearing evaluations to advanced hearing aid solutions, our Granger, IN, audiologists are here to support your hearing health with personalized care.

Hearing Services in Granger, IN

Hearing Evaluations

Your path to better hearing starts with a complete evaluation. Our audiologists use advanced testing methods to detect hearing loss and recommend personalized solutions that match your lifestyle.

Which hearing test is the best?

The best test depends on your unique needs. A full hearing evaluation by an audiologist is the most accurate way to assess your hearing. Common tests include:

  • Pure-tone audiometry – Determines the softest sounds you can hear at different pitches.
  • Speech audiometry – Measures how well you hear and understand speech.
  • Tympanometry – Evaluates middle ear function, checking for issues like fluid buildup or infections.

 

Professional evaluations give the most accurate diagnosis and guide the best treatment plan.

Can I test my own hearing?

You can look out for signs of hearing loss such as:

  • Trouble hearing in noisy places
  • Frequently asking people to repeat themselves
  • Turning the volume up too high on devices
  • Difficulty hearing high-pitched voices

 

There are also hearing test apps and kits that give you a general sense of your hearing. But they aren’t a replacement for a full evaluation. If you suspect a problem, it’s best to see an audiologist for a thorough assessment.

Are online hearing tests any good?

Online tests can offer a quick hearing check, but they have limits. These tests usually measure tone recognition through speakers or headphones, but they can’t:

  • Diagnose the type or degree of hearing loss
  • Evaluate how well you understand speech
  • Identify medical issues affecting your ears

 

Because results can be affected by noise and equipment quality, online tests should only be used as a first step. If you notice issues, schedule a full hearing evaluation with an audiologist.

Diagnostic Services

Our team offers advanced diagnostic testing to assess your hearing and detect any underlying concerns. We deliver accurate results and help you explore the right treatment options.

What are audiology diagnostic services, and why are they important?

Diagnostic services include specialized tests to evaluate hearing, ear function, and balance. These tests help identify hearing loss, tinnitus, middle ear problems, and other conditions. With a clear diagnosis, audiologists can recommend the best solution-whether it’s hearing aids, medical care, or other therapies.

What types of diagnostic tests are performed during a hearing evaluation?

A full hearing assessment may include:

  • Pure-tone audiometry – Detects the quietest sounds you can hear at different pitches.
  • Speech audiometry – Checks your ability to hear and understand words in various settings.
  • Tympanometry – Evaluates middle ear health and looks for signs of blockage or infection.
  • OAE testing – Measures inner ear response to sound, commonly used with infants and children.
  • ABR testing – Assesses how your brain processes sound signals and is helpful for neurological concerns.

 

These tests help determine your hearing level and the right course of treatment.

When should I get a diagnostic hearing test?

Consider getting tested if you have:

  • Difficulty hearing conversations, especially in noisy places
  • Ringing or buzzing in your ears (tinnitus)
  • Muffled hearing or pressure in your ears
  • Sudden or gradual hearing loss
  • Balance or dizziness issues

 

An audiologist can help identify the cause and recommend solutions.

Tinnitus Evaluations and Treatment

If you hear ringing or buzzing in your ears, our tinnitus evaluations can help find the cause. We offer effective treatments like sound therapy and coping strategies to help you manage symptoms and improve comfort.

Should I go to an audiologist for tinnitus?

Yes, audiologists are experts in tinnitus care. If you hear ringing, hissing, musical, or buzzing sounds, they can:

  • Assess your condition and potential causes
  • Check for hearing loss often linked to tinnitus
  • Recommend treatments like sound therapy or hearing aids with masking features
  • Share strategies to help reduce stress and manage symptoms

If tinnitus affects your sleep, focus, or mood, an audiologist can help you find relief.

Is tinnitus a brain problem?

Tinnitus often begins in the ears but is linked to how the brain processes sound. When hearing is reduced, the brain may try to fill the gaps by creating phantom sounds.

This is why treatments aim to reduce your awareness of the sound—rather than stopping it completely—options like hearing aids, sound therapy, or cognitive behavioral therapy (CBT) can be effective in reducing tinnitus symptoms.

What happens if tinnitus is not treated?

Tinnitus may worsen or feel more bothersome without treatment. It can lead to:

  • Increased stress or anxiety due to persistent noise
  • Trouble sleeping or focusing
  • Unmanaged hearing loss
  • Feelings of frustration or social withdrawal

Getting help early can ease symptoms and improve your daily life.

Hearing Aid Services

We provide complete hearing aid care-from choosing the right device to routine maintenance. Our services include fittings, cleanings, adjustments, and repairs to keep your hearing aids working properly.

Hearing Aid Fittings

A proper hearing aid fit ensures comfort and clear sound. We personalize your hearing aids so they match your hearing needs and feel natural in your daily life.

How long does it take to get fitted for a hearing aid?

Fitting appointments usually last one to two hours. Your audiologist will program the device, check the fit, and teach you how to use and care for it. If you need a custom earmold, it may take a couple of weeks to receive before the final fitting. Follow-up visits help fine-tune the settings for better results.

How does an audiologist fit a hearing aid?

The fitting process includes:

  • Programming the device based on your hearing test
  • Ensuring comfort and secure fit in your ear
  • Real-ear measurements (REM) to verify sound levels inside your ear canal
  • Sound testing and adjustments for clarity
  • Education on daily use and maintenance
  • Scheduling follow-ups to make improvements after wearing them in real life

Can you get a hearing aid without a test?

Over-the-counter (OTC) hearing aids are available without a test, but they are best for mild hearing loss and don’t offer a personalized fit.

A a comprehensive hearing evaluation ensures you get the right device for your needs and rules out medical issues. An audiologist can also fine-tune your hearing aid for better sound quality and comfort.

Hearing Aid Repair

If your hearing aid isn’t working properly, we offer quick and reliable repairs. Whether it’s a small issue or something more serious, our team is here to help.

What are common problems that require hearing aid repair?

Hearing aids can have occasional issues like:

  • No sound or weak volume-often due to battery or wax buildup
  • Distorted sound-may come from moisture or internal damage
  • Whistling or feedback-usually caused by poor fit or wax in the ear
  • Broken parts-like cracked cases or damaged battery doors

How long does it take to repair a hearing aid?

Simple fixes like cleaning or replacing parts can often be done in the office during your visit. More complex repairs may need to be sent to the manufacturer, which can take a few days to a few weeks.

Can I fix my hearing aid at home?

You can handle some minor problems at home by:

  • Replacing batteries
  • Cleaning parts like the microphone or tubing
  • Checking the fit and volume
  • Using a dehumidifier to remove moisture

 

For anything more serious, avoid opening the device yourself. Let a professional handle the repair to avoid further damage.

Hearing Protection

Hearing protection isn’t just for work-it’s for life. At Campbell Hearing Solutions, we provide custom-fit solutions to help you stay safe in noisy environments. Whether you’re a musician, hunter, construction worker, or just enjoy loud events, we’ll match you with hearing protection that fits comfortably and works effectively. From custom earplugs to filtered in-ear monitors, we make it easy to protect your hearing without losing clarity or enjoyment.

What’s the big deal about hearing protection?

Repeated exposure to loud noise can cause permanent hearing damage. Using the right protection helps preserve your hearing for years to come.

Why choose custom earplugs over ones from the store?

Custom earplugs are made just for your ears. That means they’re more comfortable, provide better noise reduction, and are less likely to fall out.

Will I still be able to hear people talk?

Yes! Some earplugs are designed to lower dangerous sound levels while still allowing speech to come through clearly-perfect for social or work settings.

Make an Appointment

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, our dedicated team of audiologists brings decades of experience in helping people hear and working with the latest hearing technology.

We go beyond a simple hearing test—our comprehensive evaluations assess hearing, cognition, vision, balance, and dexterity, ensuring a holistic approach to your care.

Let us help you get back to doing what you love with confidence.

The Best Way to Clean Ears: Safe Methods and Professional Solutions for Healthy Hearing

  • David A. Campbell, M.D
  • October 15, 2025

Knowing the best way to clean ears helps prevent blockage, discomfort, and hearing problems. Many people use unsafe methods like cotton swabs or ear candles, which can cause damage. At Campbell Hearing Solutions, we explain safe ear cleaning techniques, gentle at-home care, and professional solutions that keep your ears clear and healthy. 

Why Ear Cleaning Matters 

Cleaning your ears may seem simple, but doing it the wrong way can lead to earwax blockage, infection, or even damage to the ear canal. Common methods like cotton swabs or home ear candles are not safe. They can push wax deeper into the ear instead of removing it. That’s why learning the best way to clean ears is so important. 

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, we help patients understand safe ear hygiene practices, when to clean at home, and when to see a doctor or audiologist for professional help. 

How Ears Naturally Stay Clean 

Your ears have their own cleaning system. The ear canal slowly moves skin cells, wax, and small bits of debris outward. Once the wax reaches the outer ear, it flakes off naturally or washes away during showering. 

Earwax, also called cerumen, plays important roles:

  • Protects the ear from bacteria and fungi 
  • Traps dirt and dust to keep them from reaching the eardrum 
  • Keeps the ear canal moist to prevent dryness and itching 

Most of the time, your ears don’t need much help. Cleaning too often or using the wrong method can interfere with this natural process and cause irritation or wax impaction. 

Unsafe Ear Cleaning Methods to Avoid

Even though they seem harmless, some common ear cleaning techniques can do more harm than good: 

Cotton swabs (Q-tips): 

These can push wax deeper into the canal and may even puncture the eardrum. 

Ear candles:

This method is unsafe and ineffective. It can cause burns, leave melted wax inside the ear, and worsen blockage. 

Sharp or small objects: 

Using hairpins, keys, or fingernails to remove wax can injure the ear canal and increase infection risk. 

If your ears feel clogged or blocked, don’t reach for home remedies-schedule a visit with a doctor or audiologist instead. 

Safe Methods to Clean Ears at Home

If you feel the need to clean your ears, there are safe and gentle ways to do it. These methods can help with minor wax buildup but should never replace professional care. 

Warm water rinse: 

Let warm water from your shower flow gently into your outer ear. Then tilt your head to let it drain out. This can help remove light debris without irritation. 

Damp cloth: 

Use a soft, warm cloth to clean the outer ear only. Avoid inserting anything inside your ear canal. 

Ear drops or oil: 

Over-the-counter ear drops or a few drops of mineral or olive oil can help soften wax, making it easier to come out naturally. 

Rubber bulb syringe: 

If you’ve already used oil or drops to soften wax, you may gently flush your ear with warm water using a small syringe. Tilt your head to drain afterward. 

However, if you feel pain, dizziness, or notice persistent blockage after trying these steps, stop and contact a hearing specialist. 

When to See a Doctor or Audiologist 

There are times when earwax builds up faster than your body can remove it. This is called impacted earwax, and it can cause discomfort and hearing problems. 

Schedule an appointment with a doctor or audiologist if you experience: 

  • Ear fullness or blockage 
  • Sudden or gradual hearing loss 
  • Itching, ringing, or pain 
  • Discharge or unusual odor 
  • Dizziness or pressure in the ear 

Campbell Hearing Solutions provides safe, professional hearing evaluations and ear health guidance in Granger, IN.

If wax buildup is causing symptoms, a qualified medical provider can evaluate it safely. 

Professional Ear Cleaning: The Safest Solution 

If you have stubborn wax or debris that won’t come out, a professional cleaning is the best solution. At Campbell Hearing Solutions, our Granger, IN audiologists and hearing specialists use medical-grade tools and proven techniques that protect your ears while removing wax buildup. 

Our professional ear cleaning service includes: 

Detailed ear exams: 

We use an otoscope to look inside your ear canal and check for wax, fluid, or infection. Campbell Hearing Solutions provides comprehensive hearing tests in Granger, IN.

When to seek medical help: 

Using gentle suction, irrigation, or a small syringe, we carefully remove wax without harming the eardrum. 

Softening agents: 

In some cases, we apply specialized drops or oil to soften hardened wax before removal. 

Hearing check: 

If you’ve noticed hearing changes, we’ll test your hearing and recommend next steps if needed. This professional approach ensures your ears stay clear, healthy, and free from blockage. 

Why You Shouldn’t Try to Remove Deep Wax Yourself 

Trying to remove earwax at home with sharp tools or excessive flushing can push wax deeper and make things worse. In some cases, you can cause infection or injury to the ear canal. Even using a syringe or ear drops incorrectly can lead to dizziness or pain if your eardrum is damaged. That’s why it’s always safest to let a trained audiologist or doctor perform the procedure. 

Our experts at Campbell Hearing Solutions use professional methods that safely soften, loosen, and remove wax without risk. 

Caring for Your Ears After Cleaning 

Once your ears are clean, you can prevent future buildup by following these easy tips: 

Avoid frequent cleaning: 

Too much cleaning can cause irritation and dryness. 

Protect your ears from moisture: 

After swimming or bathing, gently towel off your outer ear and let water drain out. 

Keep your ears healthy: 

Don’t use hairpins, cotton swabs, or other objects inside your ears. 

Schedule regular checkups: 

A yearly visit with an audiologist ensures your ears stay clear and your hearing stays strong. 

These simple habits, combined with professional cleanings when needed, will keep your ears healthy and functioning properly. 

When Wax Build-Up Becomes a Problem 

Sometimes, earwax becomes too hard or dry to come out naturally. This can lead to discomfort, blockage, or temporary hearing loss. In these cases, an audiologist can use softening oil and gentle suction to remove it safely. 

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, we often see patients who tried at-home cleaning but still struggle with wax. We provide personalized care and use safe, medical-grade methods to restore comfort and hearing clarity. 

Professional Ear Care in Granger, IN 

Your ears are delicate and designed to protect themselves. The best way to clean ears is with safe habits at home and professional care when needed. Whether you’re experiencing wax buildup, discomfort, or muffled hearing, Campbell Hearing Solutions offers expert guidance for hearing concerns in Granger, IN. 

Our audiologists can soften hardened wax, clear blockage, and remove debris using the right tools and methods. With our expert care, you can avoid pain and prevent long-term damage. If you’ve tried cleaning your ears at home without success or just want to be sure your ears are healthy, schedule an appointment today. We’ll help you find the safest solution for clean, comfortable ears and better hearing.

Make an Appointment

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, our dedicated team of audiologists brings decades of experience in helping people hear and working with the latest hearing technology.

We go beyond a simple hearing test—our comprehensive evaluations assess hearing, cognition, vision, balance, and dexterity, ensuring a holistic approach to your care.

Let us help you get back to doing what you love with confidence.

bodyHtml:
Skip to content

Home › Costco Hearing Aids

Costco Hearing Aids

Costco has become a convenient option for many people looking to buy hearing aids. Known for big savings and one-stop shopping, many Costco stores now offer hearing tests and hearing aids. You don’t need to be a Costco member to get your hearing tested, but a membership is required to buy hearing aids.

Costco offers several well-known brands, including its own Kirkland Signature line. Some models include features like Bluetooth streaming, rechargeable batteries, and smartphone apps. It may seem like a simple solution, especially if you’re already there to shop for household goods or groceries.

But hearing loss is more than a retail decision. It’s a medical condition that deserves expert attention. 

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, our audiologists in Granger, IN provide personalized care, expert fittings, and ongoing support to help you hear your best every day, not just at checkout.

We offer a wide selection of hearing aids in Granger, IN.

What Brands Does Costco Offer?

Costco carries a limited selection of hearing aid brands, including:

  • Kirkland Signature (by Sonova)
  • Jabra Enhance (by ReSound)
  • Rexton (from the Signia family)
  • Philips HearLink

 

These models offer good technology at lower prices, but they may not come with the same level of fitting precision, follow-up care, or audiological expertise you’d receive in a clinic like ours.

What We Offer At Campbell Hearing Solutions

We work with many of the top hearing aid brands, including:

  • Phonak
  • ReSound
  • Oticon
  • Widex
  • Starkey
  • Signia

What truly sets us apart isn’t just the technology, it’s the care behind it. Our audiologists in South Bend, IN take the time to understand your lifestyle, hearing needs, and budget. 

You’ll receive a full diagnostic hearing test, a custom hearing aid fitting, and ongoing support to make sure your devices are working for you, not just sitting in a drawer.

If you need to get fitted with reliable and high-quality hearing aids in Granger, IN, please contact us for an appointment.

Is Costco the Right Place for Hearing Aids?

If cost is your biggest concern, Costco might seem like a good place to start. But it’s important to understand the trade-offs.

Most Costco hearing centers are staffed by hearing aid dispensers, not audiologists. While they can assist with basic fittings, they may not be trained to diagnose or manage more complex hearing concerns, such as tinnitus, sudden hearing loss, or inner ear conditions.

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, our Granger, IN audiologists provide complete hearing healthcare, from diagnosis to long-term follow-up, so you can feel confident that your hearing is in expert hands.

Costco vs. Campbell Hearing Solutions: A Side-by-Side Look

Costco Offers:

  • Basic hearing screenings
  • A few hearing aid models
  • Limited follow-up care
  • Retail-based environment

 

Campbell Hearing Solutions Offers:

  • Comprehensive hearing evaluations
  • Custom programming and real-ear measurements
  • Tinnitus management and counseling
  • Local follow-ups and adjustments
  • A compassionate, clinical setting
  • Accessories, repairs, and battery support

What Else Should You Consider?
  • Upfront Cost vs. Long-Term Value: Costco may advertise lower prices, but their services often don’t include personalized fittings, counseling, or ongoing check-ins. At our clinic, those services are built in, because your hearing health isn’t one-size-fits-all.
  • Warranty & Repairs: Costco warranties can vary by brand. At Campbell Hearing Solutions, we walk you through all warranty details and provide local repairs and adjustments. No waiting, no shipping your devices away.
  • Accessories & Support: We offer a full range of hearing accessories, including TV streamers, remote microphones, and smartphone connectivity. Our South Bend, IN audiologists are here to help you set them up and use them effectively.

Do Costco Hearing Centers Have Audiologists?

In most cases, no. Costco hearing centers are generally staffed by hearing aid specialists, not licensed audiologists. At Campbell Hearing Solutions, our team includes highly trained audiologists who provide thorough evaluations, accurate fittings, and ongoing medical-grade care.

Hearing Aids in Granger, IN

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, you’re not just another shopper, you’re a valued patient. If you’re ready to treat your hearing loss with expert care and advanced technology, we’re here to help every step of the way.

You can rely on our licensed Granger, IN audiologists trained to identify, treat, and manage hearing conditions, ensuring you receive the highest level of care.

We offer a wide selection of hearing aids in Granger, IN to ensure that you get access to the best hearing solutions.

Contact us today to schedule an appointment! 

Make an Appointment

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, our dedicated team of audiologists brings decades of experience in helping people hear and working with the latest hearing technology.

We go beyond a simple hearing test—our comprehensive evaluations assess hearing, cognition, vision, balance, and dexterity, ensuring a holistic approach to your care.

Let us help you get back to doing what you love with confidence.

Book Appointment

Crackling in Ear: What It Means, Common Causes, and When to Get Help

  • David A. Campbell, M.D
  • January 23, 2026
  • Crackling in the ear is often caused by pressure problems, earwax blockage, or fluid in the middle ear. Many cases are common and treatable.
  • Simple home remedies may help at first, but ongoing crackling, discomfort, or earache should be checked by a professional.
  • An audiologist can find the cause of crackling in the ear and help prevent hearing loss, tinnitus, or infection.

Hearing a crackling in your ear can feel strange and worrying. Some people notice it when they swallow, yawn, or move their jaw. Others hear crackling sounds throughout the day, even when sitting still. While it can be harmless at times, crackling in the ear can also be a sign that something inside the ear is not working as it should.

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, we often encounter patients who are unsure if crackling in the ear is serious or just a passing issue. This guide explains the most common causes, simple ways to find relief, and when it’s time to see an audiologist or get a hearing test for answers.

What Does Crackling in the Ear Feel Like?

Crackling in the ear does not feel the same for everyone. You might notice:

  • Crackling or popping sounds
  • A feeling of fullness or blockage
  • Muffled hearing
  • Pressure that comes and goes
  • Mild earache or discomfort
  • Crackling when chewing or moving your jaw

Some people also notice ringing sounds, known as tinnitus, along with crackling. These symptoms may appear together or on their own.

Why Crackling in the Ear Happens

Your ears are made up of small, delicate parts that must work together. When one part is blocked, swollen, or irritated, it can cause crackling sounds.

Eustachian Tube Dysfunction

One of the most common causes of crackling in the ear is Eustachian tube dysfunction. The Eustachian tubes are small passages that connect the middle ear to the back of the nose and throat. They help:

  • Balance air pressure
  • Drain fluid
  • Keep the ear comfortable

When these tubes cannot open and close properly, air becomes trapped. This trapped air causes pressure, popping, and crackling in the ear.

Common triggers include:

  • Colds and sinus infections
  • Allergies
  • Changes in weather or humidity
  • Flying or altitude changes

Earwax Blockage

Earwax protects your ears, but too much earwax can cause a blockage. When wax builds up, it can block sound and movement inside the ear canal.

Signs earwax may be the cause include:

  • Crackling sounds
  • Muffled hearing
  • A plugged or full feeling
  • Ringing or tinnitus

Trying to remove earwax at home with cotton swabs can make the blockage worse. A medical evaluation is the safest next step.

Fluid in the Middle Ear

Fluid behind the eardrum often develops after a cold, allergy flare-up, or sinus infection. Even without pain, fluid can cause:

  • Crackling or bubbling sounds
  • Pressure or fullness
  • Temporary hearing changes

If fluid stays too long, it may lead to discomfort, earache, or infection.

Ear Infections and Crackling

An ear infection can also cause crackling in the ear. Infections often come with:

  • Earache or pain
  • Warmth or pressure in the ear
  • Drainage
  • Hearing changes

If crackling is paired with pain or fever, it is important to see a doctor or audiologist right away.

Jaw Problems (TMJ)

The jaw joint sits close to the ear. Problems with this joint, called TMJ disorders, can cause sounds that feel like they are coming from the ear.

Crackling linked to jaw issues may happen:

  • When chewing
  • When opening the mouth wide
  • With jaw pain or stiffness

An audiologist can help decide if the ear or jaw is the main cause.

Campbell Hearing Solutions connects you to an expert audiologist in Granger, IN.

Pressure Changes From Travel

Crackling in the ear is common during:

  • Airplane takeoff and landing
  • Driving in hilly or mountain areas
  • Elevator rides in tall buildings

These pressure changes make the ears work harder to equalize. Crackling usually goes away once pressure settles, but repeated problems may signal tube issues.

Is Crackling in the Ear Dangerous?

Most of the time, crackling in the ear is not dangerous, but it should still be watched. You should schedule a hearing check if crackling:

  • Lasts more than a week
  • Happens often
  • Comes with hearing loss
  • Causes pain, dizziness, or balance issues
  • Occurs in only one ear

Early care can prevent bigger problems later.

Simple At-Home Steps That May Help

For mild crackling in the ear, these gentle steps may help:

  • Swallow, yawn, or chew sugar-free gum
  • Drink water to stay hydrated
  • Use a warm compress near the ear
  • Take steamy showers to loosen congestion
  • Rest if you are sick

Avoid forcing your ears to “pop,” as this can irritate the eardrum.

When to Avoid At-Home Treatment

Do not try home remedies if you have:

  • Ear pain
  • Drainage or fluid from the ear
  • Sudden hearing loss
  • Dizziness or spinning

These signs need professional care.

How an Audiologist Can Help

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, we take crackling in the ear seriously. An audiologist can:

  • Look inside your ears safely
  • Check for earwax buildup
  • Test middle-ear pressure
  • Perform hearing tests
  • Find the true cause of crackling

Once the cause is clear, we can recommend the right treatment plan.

Treatment Options for Crackling in the Ear

Treatment depends on the cause. Options may include:

Professional Ear Evaluation

A qualified medical provider can determine whether wax buildup is part of the problem and recommend the right treatment.

Middle-Ear Testing

This checks how well the eardrum and middle ear are moving.

Allergy or Sinus Care Guidance

Reducing swelling can help the ears open naturally.

Hearing Monitoring

If hearing changes are found, we track them over time.

Referrals When Needed

If a medical issue is suspected, we guide you to the right provider.

Crackling in the Ear and Hearing Loss

Sometimes crackling in the ear is an early sign of hearing changes. Even mild hearing loss can affect how sound travels through the ear.

A hearing test can:

  • Measure hearing levels
  • Check how well you hear speech
  • Spot early changes before they worsen

Early detection leads to better outcomes.

Children and Crackling in the Ear

Kids can also experience crackling in the ear, often from:

  • Ear infections
  • Fluid buildup
  • Frequent colds

If a child complains of ear sounds, trouble hearing, or ear fullness, a hearing check is important.

Preventing Crackling in the Ear

While not all causes can be prevented, these habits help protect ear health:

  • Avoid cotton swabs in the ear canal
  • Treat colds and allergies early
  • Wear hearing protection in loud places
  • Dry ears gently after swimming or bathing
  • Get routine hearing checks

When to Schedule a Hearing Evaluation

Make an appointment if you notice:

  • Ongoing crackling in the ear
  • Hearing that feels muffled
  • Trouble understanding speech
  • Ringing or buzzing sounds
  • Ear pressure that will not go away

These signs are your body’s way of asking for help.

Audiologists and Hearing Tests in Granger, IN

If you are dealing with crackling in the ear, you do not have to guess what’s causing it. At Campbell Hearing Solutions, we provide caring, thorough hearing evaluations for adults and children.

We focus on:

  • Clear answers
  • Gentle care
  • Personalized treatment plans

Whether your crackling is due to earwax, pressure, or middle-ear changes, we are here to help you feel comfortable and confident again.

Get Your Hearing Tested Today

Crackling in the ear is common, but it should never be ignored if it keeps coming back. Understanding the cause is the first step toward relief. With the right care, most people find their symptoms improve and their hearing stays healthy.

If crackling in the ear is affecting your comfort or peace of mind, schedule a visit with Campbell Hearing Solutions in Granger, IN today. Getting answers now can protect your hearing for years to come.

Make an Appointment

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, our dedicated team of audiologists brings decades of experience in helping people hear and working with the latest hearing technology.

We go beyond a simple hearing test—our comprehensive evaluations assess hearing, cognition, vision, balance, and dexterity, ensuring a holistic approach to your care.

Let us help you get back to doing what you love with confidence.

Home › Custom Hearing Protection

Custom Hearing Protection

Campbell Hearing Solutions provides custom hearing protection in Granger, IN.

Protecting your hearing is essential-whether you’re working in a noisy environment, enjoying live music, hunting, swimming, or simply trying to sleep peacefully.

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, our experienced Granger, IN audiologists provide custom hearing protection that’s built for comfort, safety, and long-term ear health.

Whether you’re a musician, hunter, industrial worker, or light sleeper, we offer both ready-made and custom-fit options, including earplugs, earmuffs, and advanced SoundGear digital hearing protection.

We will help you choose the best solution for your needs and lifestyle.

Types of Hearing Protection

Ready-Made Hearing Protection

These pre-molded options are convenient and budget-friendly, though they may not offer the best comfort or fit for long-term use.

  • Earplugs – Inserted into the ear canal.
    • Pros: Affordable and portable.
    • Cons: May provide less comfort and a less secure fit.
  • Semi-Insert Earplugs – Connected by a band and rest at the entrance of the ear canal.
    • Pros: Reusable and easy to wear.
    • Cons: Lower noise reduction.
  • Earmuffs – Cover the entire ear to block outside noise.
    • Pros: Strong overall protection.
    • Cons: Can feel bulky or uncomfortable with extended use.

Custom Hearing Protection

Made using impressions of your ears, custom protection offers a superior fit, better noise reduction, and all-day comfort.

  • Custom Earplugs – Great for musicians, workers, and everyday protection.
    • Pros: Comfortable, durable, and secure.
    • Cons: Requires a custom fitting.
  • Impulse Filter Earplugs – Designed for hunters to block sudden loud sounds like gunfire while allowing softer ambient sounds.
    • Pros: Smart protection.
    • Cons: Not intended for continuous loud noise.
  • Sleep Plugs – Soft, flexible earplugs made for nighttime use.
    • Pros: Ideal for side sleepers and blocking noise.
    • Cons: May reduce awareness of alarms or essential sounds.
  • In-Ear Monitors – High-fidelity devices used by musicians and performers.
    • Pros: Outstanding sound clarity.
    • Cons: Higher cost compared to standard plugs.
  • SoundGear Digital Protection – Digital hearing protection that reduces loud noises while preserving clarity.
    • Pros: Ideal for shooting and noisy environments.
    • Cons: Premium cost.

Hearing Protection for Every Activity

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, our Granger, IN audiologists provide custom hearing protection tailored to your daily life:

  • Hunting – Impulse filter earplugs and SoundGear options help block gunfire while keeping you aware of your surroundings.
  • Music – Custom musicians’ plugs and in-ear monitors reduce volume safely while maintaining sound quality.
  • Swimming – Custom swim plugs prevent water from entering the ears and help avoid infections like swimmer’s ear.
  • Sleeping – Sleep plugs reduce outside noise for better rest.
  • Work & Industry – Whether in construction or manufacturing, custom plugs and earmuffs provide dependable protection.
  • Water Sports – Surf and swim plugs shield your ears from cold water and wind, helping prevent surfer’s ear.

Types of Custom Ear Molds

We offer several material options for your custom ear molds:

  • Vinyl – Soft and flexible, perfect for children or sensitive ears.
    • Pros: Comfortable and hypoallergenic.
    • Cons: Less durable over time.
  • Silicone – Water-resistant, flexible, and ideal for active users.
    • Pros: Long-lasting and durable.
    • Cons: Harder to adjust once molded.
  • Acrylic – Hard, lightweight, and easy to clean.
    • Pros: Great clarity and ease of use.
    • Cons: May not be best for those with sensitive ears.

 

Custom molds can be used for hearing aids, swimming, sleeping, or sound protection.

How Are Custom Ear Molds Made?

Our Granger, IN audiologists begin with a quick and painless ear impression. That impression is then sent to a lab where your chosen material is used to craft a mold that fits your ear perfectly. Custom molds provide a better seal, increased comfort, and greater protection compared to generic options.

How Long Do Custom Earplugs Last?

With proper care, custom earplugs can last 3 to 5 years. Their lifespan depends on how often they’re used and the material. Regular cleaning and periodic checkups help ensure a good fit-especially for growing children or adults with ear shape changes.

Custom Hearing Protection in Granger, IN

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, we’re dedicated to helping you protect your hearing for life. Whether you need protection for work, music, hunting, swimming, or sleep, our Granger, IN audiologists will guide you to the perfect custom-fit solution.

Schedule your hearing protection consultation today and take the first step toward safer, more comfortable listening.

Make an Appointment

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, our dedicated team of audiologists brings decades of experience in helping people hear and working with the latest hearing technology.

We go beyond a simple hearing test—our comprehensive evaluations assess hearing, cognition, vision, balance, and dexterity, ensuring a holistic approach to your care.

Let us help you get back to doing what you love with confidence.

Book Appointment

Home › Diagnostic Services in Granger, IN

Diagnostic Services in Granger, IN

Campbell Hearing Solutions has audiologists providing diagnostic services in Granger, IN.

Diagnostic hearing tests are specialized evaluations that assess your hearing ability and detect any underlying conditions affecting your ears. These tests help determine the type, severity, and cause of hearing loss, allowing audiologists to recommend the best treatment options, whether it’s hearing aids, medical intervention, or other hearing solutions.

Tinnitus

Tinnitus is the perception of ringing, buzzing, hissing, or other sounds in the ears that aren’t caused by any external source. If you’re experiencing ongoing tinnitus, a professional evaluation can help determine the underlying cause and explore treatment options.

During your visit, the audiologist will discuss your symptoms, perform a hearing test, and use specialized tools to evaluate how tinnitus affects your hearing and daily activities. Depending on the results, treatment may include sound therapy, hearing aids with tinnitus masking features, or relaxation and coping strategies.

If tinnitus is impacting your quality of life, schedule an evaluation to find relief and support.

Hearing Loss

Hearing loss occurs when sounds become difficult to hear or understand. It can range from mild to profound and may affect one or both ears. Some common signs include:

  • Trouble understanding speech, especially in noisy environments
  • Frequently asking others to repeat themselves
  • Needing to turn up the TV or phone volume
  • Hearing, ringing, musical, or buzzing sounds (tinnitus)

 

A hearing evaluation by an audiologist is the most effective way to determine the cause and degree of hearing loss. The process typically includes:

  • A review of your medical history and symptoms
  • A physical exam of your ears to check for blockages or infections
  • Hearing tests to measure your ability to hear different sounds and speech

Sensorineural Hearing Loss

Sensorineural hearing loss (SNHL) occurs when there is damage to the inner ear (cochlea) or the auditory nerve. This type of hearing loss is permanent and affects how sound signals are transmitted to the brain.

Common symptoms of SNHL include:

  • Speech sounds that seem muffled or unclear
  • Difficulty understanding conversations, especially in noisy areas
  • Trouble hearing high-frequency sounds like birds or alarms
  • Ringing or buzzing in the ears (tinnitus)

 

An audiologist can diagnose SNHL through a variety of tests, including:

  • Hearing tests – To assess how well you hear different pitches and volumes
  • Speech audiometry – To evaluate speech understanding at various levels
  • Tympanometry – To check middle ear function and rule out other issues

Sudden Sensorineural Hearing Loss

Sudden sensorineural hearing loss (SSNHL), also known as sudden deafness, is a quick and unexplained loss of hearing that occurs within a short time-usually in one ear. It’s considered a medical emergency and should be evaluated right away.

Common symptoms of SSNHL include:

  • Rapid hearing loss in one ear
  • A sensation of fullness or pressure in the affected ear
  • Dizziness or problems with balance
  • Ringing or buzzing sounds (tinnitus)

 

An audiologist will conduct:

  • A hearing test (audiogram) to assess your current hearing levels
  • Tympanometry to evaluate how your middle ear is functioning

 

An Audiologist will perform a hearing examination to confirm sudden hearing loss and may refer you to a doctor for medical intervention.

Diagnostic Services in Granger, IN

Hearing health is important for your overall well-being. At Campbell Hearing Solutions, we provide expert diagnostic services in Granger, IN, to help you hear your best.

Whether you’re noticing changes in your hearing or just want a routine check-up, our Granger, IN, audiologists are here to help.

Schedule your hearing test today!

Make an Appointment

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, our dedicated team of audiologists brings decades of experience in helping people hear and working with the latest hearing technology.

We go beyond a simple hearing test—our comprehensive evaluations assess hearing, cognition, vision, balance, and dexterity, ensuring a holistic approach to your care.

Let us help you get back to doing what you love with confidence.

Home › Hearing Aids › Ear Candles: What You Need to Know

Ear Candles: What You Need to Know

  • David A. Campbell, M.D
  • November 8, 2025

Ear candling is a trendy alternative earcare technique that some people use to try to cleanse or remove ear wax. It involves placing a hollow, cone-shaped candle into the ear and lighting the other end. As the flame burns, it’s often marketed as a soothing and “holistic” ritual that uses heat and supposed “natural” treatment to draw ear wax out and support overall wellness and balance.

However, ear candling is not only ineffective, it can also be dangerous.

Ear wax is not dirty or harmful. In fact, it’s an important part of your ear’s natural auditory hygiene system. It helps cleanse the ear canal, traps dust, protects against infection, and usually works its way out on its own. When wax builds up or causes discomfort, it’s always best to see a hearing professional instead of trying DIY therapy or “alternative” methods like ear candles.

Campbell Hearing Solutions provides professional medical evaluation in Granger, IN, using safe, medically approved methods for true wellness and hearing balance.

How Are Ear Candles Used?

Ear candling is often promoted as a relaxing, spa-like ritual, but the practice is misleading and unsafe. It involves placing a fabric tube soaked in wax into your ear canal and lighting the exposed end. As it burns, supporters claim it creates a vacuum that pulls out earwax and toxins. Some even say it offers relaxation and “energy balance” as part of a holistic treatment.

In reality, research shows this suction effect doesn’t happen. Any residue inside the candle is from the burned wax, not from inside your ear. The heat from the flame can cause burns, and melted wax can drip into the ear canal, making the blockage worse. In some cases, the technique has even caused punctured eardrums.

What’s marketed as a “natural” therapy for auditory wellness can actually damage your hearing.

What Do Experts Say About Ear Candling?

Medical experts strongly advise against using ear candles. Numerous studies have found no evidence that they remove earwax or offer any real treatment benefits. There is no proven therapeutic effect, only risk.

Professional organizations, including the American Academy of Audiology and the American Academy of Otolaryngology–Head and Neck Surgery, state that ear candling poses more danger than benefit. Reported injuries include:

  • Burns to the face or ear canal from the flame and heat
  • Dripping wax that blocks the ear canal instead of helping cleanse it
  • Perforated eardrums from incorrect placement

The U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) has also issued warnings about ear candles. They are not approved medical devices, have no proven health or wellness benefits, and are not considered safe treatment for earwax or any auditory problems.

How Can I Remove Wax Safely?

It’s understandable to want a quick, “natural” fix when your ears feel blocked, but ear candling is a risky practice that can seriously harm your ears. Here’s a look at the pros and cons of ear candles:

Pros (as marketed):

  • Easy to find online or in stores
  • Promoted as “natural” and “holistic” earcare

Cons (in reality):

  • No scientific proof they work
  • Risk of burns and injuries from flame and heat
  • Can push wax deeper, worsening the problem
  • Not FDA approved
  • Expensive and ineffective as a treatment

Instead of relying on unsafe alternative methods, trust your earcare to experts who use safe, evidence-based techniques such as:

  • Otoscopic ear examination to assess wax buildup
  • Manual removal using gentle tools
  • Water irrigation or suction, when appropriate

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, these methods are designed to protect your hearing and support true auditory wellness, not just offer a temporary or risky “ritual.”

What Are Safer Methods to Clean Our Ears?

Most of the time, your ears do a great job of hygiene and self-cleansing without any special treatment. You don’t need a candle, flame, or heat to keep them healthy. Here are some safer ways to support ear health:

  • Let ear wax work its way out naturally; it often falls out during showering or sleeping.
  • Use a warm, damp cloth to gently cleanse the outer ear.
  • Avoid cotton swabs. They can push wax deeper and damage the ear canal.
  • Use earwax-softening drops (such as mineral oil or hydrogen peroxide) if you feel buildup.
  • Visit an audiologist if you notice discomfort, hearing loss, itching, or a feeling of auditory balance being “off.”

These options support healthy earcare and hygiene without the risks associated with ear candling.

If wax buildup is causing discomfort, a qualified medical provider can recommend safe, effective treatment that protects your hearing and supports overall wellness.

Schedule an Appointment Today

When it comes to your ears, safety should always come before any trendy alternative ritual. Ear candles may look relaxing, and the warm heat and flame might sound soothing, but medical experts agree: ear candling is ineffective and risky. Whether it’s burns, blockages, or damage to your eardrum, this practice simply isn’t worth the risk.

If you’re experiencing wax buildup or discomfort, the best “holistic” choice for your long-term wellness and auditory balance is professional care, not a candle.

Campbell Hearing Solutions provides guidance from a qualified medical provider. Don’t risk your hearing with unsafe home techniques. Schedule an appointment today and let us provide truly soothing, expert earcare so you can hear clearly and comfortably.

Make an Appointment

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, our dedicated team of audiologists brings decades of experience in helping people hear and working with the latest hearing technology.

We go beyond a simple hearing test—our comprehensive evaluations assess hearing, cognition, vision, balance, and dexterity, ensuring a holistic approach to your care.

Let us help you get back to doing what you love with confidence.

bodyHtml:
Skip to content

Home › Hearing Aid Alternatives: Assistive Listening Devices

Hearing Aid Alternatives: Assistive Listening Devices

Even with high-quality hearing aids, certain situations can still be challenging. Large gatherings, noisy rooms, or watching TV from across the room can make hearing difficult. That’s why our audiologists offer assistive listening devices (ALDs) designed to help you hear better in those tricky moments.

Whether you’re trying to catch a conversation in a crowded restaurant, enjoy your favorite show without cranking up the volume, or follow along in a community meeting, ALDs can bring clarity and comfort to your hearing experience.

What’s the difference between hearing aids and assistive listening devices?

Hearing aids are great for daily use. They’re custom-fit and programmed to meet your unique hearing needs. But sometimes, even the best hearing aids can use a little backup-especially in situations with background noise or distance between you and the sound.

That’s where assistive listening devices come in. ALDs are simple, task-focused tools that make sounds louder and clearer in specific settings. They can work on their own for those with mild hearing loss or be used alongside hearing aids for extra support.

ALDs don’t require a hearing test or custom fitting. They’re often more affordable and easy to use, making them a helpful and practical option for many people.

Types of Assistive Listening Devices We Offer

Hearing Loop (Audio Induction) Systems

Hearing loops send sound directly to hearing aids or cochlear implants equipped with a telecoil (T-coil) using electromagnetic signals.

Benefits:

  • Less background noise
  • Ideal for churches, theaters, and large venues
  • Direct wireless connection to compatible hearing devices

Infrared Systems

Infrared systems use invisible light waves to deliver sound to a headset or neck loop.

Benefits:

  • Great for watching TV or having private conversations
  • Works well in courtrooms and home environments
  • No radio or electrical interference

FM Systems

FM systems use radio waves to send a speaker’s voice from a microphone to the listener’s receiver.

Benefits:

  • Perfect for classrooms, group settings, or public speaking events
  • Reduces the impact of background noise and distance
  • Can be used with hearing aids and cochlear implants

Personal Amplified Systems

Handheld or portable devices with headphones or earbuds that amplify nearby sound.

Benefits:

  • Easy to use and budget-friendly
  • Ideal for one-on-one conversations or TV-watching
  • No fitting or hearing test needed

TV Listening Systems

These systems send TV audio to wireless headphones or compatible devices.

Benefits:

  • Clear sound without increasing volume for others
  • Better dialogue understanding
  • Bluetooth, infrared, and RF options available

Alerting Devices

These devices use lights, vibrations, or loud alerts to signal things like alarms, doorbells, or phone calls.

Benefits:

  • Improves safety and awareness
  • Helpful for those with moderate to severe hearing loss
  • Can connect with smartphones and smart home devices

Assistive Listening Devices FAQs

What is the most commonly used assistive listening device?

FM systems are widely used—especially in schools, lecture halls, and large meeting spaces. They send sound directly from the speaker’s microphone to the listener’s receiver, reducing background noise and improving clarity, even at a distance.

Why are assistive listening devices necessary?

Even with hearing aids, some places are just harder to hear in—like crowded rooms or big halls. ALDs help by focusing and boosting sound in those specific situations, helping you stay engaged, independent, and confident.

Can assistive listening devices replace hearing aids?

For those with mild hearing loss, ALDs may be helpful on their own. But for moderate to severe hearing loss, hearing aids are still essential. Most people benefit from using both—hearing aids for everyday use and ALDs for extra clarity in certain settings.

Hear Better in Every Situation

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, we believe everyone deserves to enjoy the moments that matter most. Our Granger, IN audiologists are here to help you find the best tools for your hearing needs-including assistive listening devices that make a real difference in your daily life.

Visit our Granger, IN office, or give us a call today to learn more about how ALDs can help you stay connected and confident-wherever you go.

Make an Appointment

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, our dedicated team of audiologists brings decades of experience in helping people hear and working with the latest hearing technology.

We go beyond a simple hearing test—our comprehensive evaluations assess hearing, cognition, vision, balance, and dexterity, ensuring a holistic approach to your care.

Let us help you get back to doing what you love with confidence.

Home › Hearing Aid Batteries

Hearing Aid Batteries

Campbell Hearing Solutions provides hearing aid batteries in Granger, IN to ensure that your hearing devices are always functioning at their best.

Hearing aid batteries are what keep your hearing aids working. Without the right battery, your hearing aids won’t perform well or last through the day. The type of battery you use can affect both how well your hearing aids work and how long they stay powered. Choosing the right battery helps with convenience, performance, and cost.

Two Types of Hearing Aid Batteries

There are two main types of hearing aid batteries: rechargeable batteries and standard disposable batteries. Each has its own advantages.

Rechargeable Batteries

Rechargeable batteries are built into your hearing aids and don’t need to be replaced often. You simply place your hearing aids in a charging dock or case at night, and they’re fully powered by morning.

Benefits of rechargeable batteries:

  • No need to regularly buy and replace batteries
  • Easy to recharge overnight
  • Environmentally friendly-less waste
  • Great for those who have trouble handling small items

 

Keep in mind: rechargeable hearing aids need to be charged daily. If you forget to charge them, they won’t work until you recharge.

Standard Disposable Batteries

Disposable hearing aid batteries, also called zinc-air batteries, are small and round. They start working when you remove a sticker that lets air in.

Benefits of disposable batteries:

  • Easy to replace when they run out
  • Useful when a power source isn’t available
  • Battery life varies based on size and hearing aid type

 

Disposable batteries are a good choice for people who travel often or don’t always have access to a charger. However, they do need to be replaced regularly, which adds to long-term cost.

Sizes of Disposable Hearing Aid Batteries

Disposable batteries come in different sizes to fit various types of hearing aids:

  • Size 10 – Smallest battery, used in tiny hearing aids; lasts about 3-5 days
  • Size 312 – Common in behind-the-ear (BTE) and in-the-ear (ITE) styles; lasts about 5-7 days
  • Size 13 – Slightly larger, used in stronger hearing aids; lasts about 6-14 days
  • Size 675 – Largest battery, used in high-powered devices; lasts about 9-20 days

Color Codes for Battery Sizes

To help you choose the right battery size, each type is color-coded on the packaging:

  • Size 10 – Yellow
  • Size 312 – Brown
  • Size 13 – Orange
  • Size 675 – Blue

How Long Do Hearing Aid Batteries Last?

Battery life depends on the size of the battery, the type of hearing aid, and how often you use it.

  • Small batteries: 3-7 days
  • Larger batteries: 6-20 days
  • Rechargeable batteries: usually last one full day, then need to be recharged nightly

 

Using features like Bluetooth or streaming can drain batteries faster.

Tips to Make Hearing Aid Batteries Last Longer

Proper Storage and Handling:

  • Keep batteries in a cool, dry place
  • Don’t store them in the refrigerator
  • Keep them away from metal objects (keys, coins) to avoid short-circuiting

 

Activating Zinc-Air Batteries:

  • Remove the sticker only when you’re ready to use the battery
  • Let it sit for 1–2 minutes before inserting it into the hearing aid—this helps activate it fully

 

Caring for Rechargeable Batteries:

  • Charge your hearing aids every night
  • Avoid overcharging—always use the charger made for your hearing aids
  • If storing hearing aids for a long time, turn them off to save battery life

When to Replace Your Batteries

You may need new batteries if:

  • Sounds become faint or unclear
  • Your hearing aid stops working
  • You hear beeping sounds (a low battery warning)

Hearing Aid Batteries in Granger, IN

Need hearing aid batteries?

Campbell Hearing Solutions offers a wide range of hearing aid batteries in Granger, IN. Whether you need rechargeable batteries or standard disposable ones, we can help you find the right fit for your hearing aids.

Our Granger, IN audiologists are ready to assist you in choosing the best option for your needs.

Visit or contact us to find the right batteries for your hearing aids!

Hearing Aid Fittings in Granger, IN

Campbell Hearing Solutions provides expert hearing aid fittings in Granger, IN.

A hearing aid fitting is when a hearing care professional adjusts your hearing aids to meet your unique hearing needs. The goal is to help you hear clearly and comfortably-not just make things louder. Your hearing aids need to be fine-tuned to match your specific type of hearing loss for the best experience.

Why Is a Professional Hearing Aid Fitting Important?

A proper fitting ensures your hearing aids work their best. Audiologists are trained to adjust your devices so speech sounds crisp and background noise doesn’t overwhelm you. With a good fit, everyday sounds feel more natural and comfortable.

Why Should an Audiologist Fit Your Hearing Aids?

Audiologists specialize in hearing health. They know how to properly fit and adjust hearing aids so they work just right. Poorly fitted hearing aids can feel uncomfortable or make sounds too loud or too soft. A professional fitting ensures your hearing aids are tailored to your needs.

What Happens During a Hearing Aid Fitting?

Step-by-Step Process:

  • Reviewing Your Hearing Test – The audiologist explains your hearing loss and how hearing aids can help.
  • Selecting the Right Hearing Aids – Your audiologist helps you choose the best style and features for your lifestyle.
  • Programming the Hearing Aids – Special software is used to adjust your hearing aids based on your hearing test results.
  • Real Ear Measurement (REM) – A small microphone is placed in your ear to make sure the hearing aids are amplifying sound correctly.
  • Instruction and Care – You’ll learn how to insert, remove, and care for your hearing aids.
  • Scheduling Follow-Up Visits – Your audiologist will schedule follow-up appointments to fine-tune your hearing aids for maximum comfort and performance.

What to Expect at Your First Fitting

Your first hearing aid fitting is an exciting step toward better hearing. You may notice new sounds right away-some may seem loud at first, but your brain will adapt. Your audiologist will answer your questions and make sure your devices fit comfortably.

What Is Real Ear Measurement (REM)?

Real Ear Measurement is a special test that ensures your hearing aids are set correctly for your ears. A small microphone goes into your ear to measure how sound is being delivered.

Why Real Ear Measurement Matters:

Everyone’s ears are shaped differently. REM helps your audiologist adjust your hearing aids so they give you the best sound possible. Without it, the hearing aids might not perform as well as they should.

Adjusting to Your Hearing Aids

What to Expect:

At first, you may hear things you haven’t heard in a while-like clocks ticking or footsteps. That’s normal! Your brain is relearning how to hear those sounds.

Tips for Getting Used to Hearing Aids:
  • Start wearing your hearing aids a few hours at a time.
  • Gradually increase wear time each day.
  • Practice listening in quiet settings before going into noisy environments.
  • Be patient-it may take a few weeks to fully adjust.

Why Follow-Up Appointments Matter

Follow-up visits are key to success. If your hearing aids feel uncomfortable or don’t sound quite right, your audiologist can make changes. These adjustments help you get the most out of your devices.

Hearing Aid Fitting FAQs

Are hearing aid fittings covered by insurance?

Coverage varies by insurance provider. Medicare usually doesn’t cover hearing aids, but some private plans or VA benefits may help. Check with your provider to find out what’s included.

What if my hearing aid doesn’t fit well?

If your hearing aid feels uncomfortable, your audiologist can make adjustments. Different ear tips or custom earmolds can improve the fit. Never try to force it in-it could cause discomfort or affect sound quality.

How long does a hearing aid fitting take?

The first fitting usually takes about an hour. However, it often takes a few follow-up visits to fine-tune the fit and sound settings for the best results.

Hearing Aid Fittings in Granger, IN

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, we are committed to helping you hear better with expert hearing aid fittings. Our audiologists take the time to ensure your hearing aids are comfortable and provide the best sound quality.

If you or a loved one needs a hearing aid fitting, schedule an appointment with Campbell Hearing Solutions today!

Call us or visit our website to get started on your journey to better hearing.

Make an Appointment

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, our dedicated team of audiologists brings decades of experience in helping people hear and working with the latest hearing technology.

We go beyond a simple hearing test—our comprehensive evaluations assess hearing, cognition, vision, balance, and dexterity, ensuring a holistic approach to your care.

Let us help you get back to doing what you love with confidence.

Hearing Aid Services in Granger, IN

Campbell Hearing Solutions has audiologists providing expert hearing aid services in Granger, IN.

If you or a loved one is experiencing hearing loss, choosing the right hearing aid is an important step toward better communication and overall well-being. Campbell Hearing Solutions provides expert, personalized care to help you find and adjust hearing aids that suit your unique needs.

Our services include hearing evaluations, hearing aid selection, custom fittings, adjustments, and repairs. We’re committed to improving your hearing and enhancing your quality of life with compassionate, professional support.

Let our audiologists in Granger, IN, be your partner in better hearing.

Benefits of Expert Hearing Aid Services

  • Improved Communication – Hear conversations more clearly in everyday environments.
  • Enhanced Quality of Life – Enjoy music, conversations, and social events with confidence.
  • Better Cognitive Health – Addressing hearing loss may help reduce the risk of memory and concentration problems.
  • Reduced Social Isolation – Stay connected with the people and moments that matter most.

Hearing Aid Selection

Finding the right hearing aid depends on your hearing loss, lifestyle, and budget. Our team at Campbell Hearing Solutions in Granger, IN will walk you through the process, explaining the features, benefits, and styles available. We take the time to understand your goals and help you choose the best solution for your needs.

Hearing Assessments

The first step to better hearing is a thorough hearing evaluation. This helps identify the type and degree of hearing loss. Common tests include:

  • Pure-Tone Audiometry – Measures how well you hear different tones and pitches.
  • Speech Audiometry – Evaluates how clearly you understand speech in quiet and noisy settings.
  • Tympanometry – Assesses the health of your eardrum and middle ear function.

Hearing Aid Styles

Hearing aids come in different styles to suit various hearing needs and preferences:

  • Completely-in-the-Canal (CIC): Very small and discreet, best for mild to moderate hearing loss.
  • In-the-Canal (ITC): Sits partially in the ear canal.
  • In-the-Ear (ITE): Fits into the outer ear and is easier to handle.
  • Behind-the-Ear (BTE): Worn behind the ear with a small tube to the ear canal.

Hearing Aid Fitting

Once your hearing aid is selected, we ensure a personalized and secure fit. Our team takes impressions of your ear and programs the device specifically for your hearing needs, ensuring both comfort and optimal sound quality.

Hearing Aid Programming and Adjustments

Hearing needs can change over time. We offer expert adjustments to your hearing aids to improve sound clarity, reduce background noise, and enhance comfort. Whether you’re experiencing changes in your hearing or simply need a few fine-tuned tweaks, we’re here to help.

Counseling and Education

Learning how to use and care for your hearing aids is essential. We provide one-on-one guidance on:

  • Communication tips
  • Device care and maintenance
  • Managing hearing loss in daily life

 

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, we make sure you feel confident with your hearing devices and are supported every step of the way.

Real Ear Measurements

Real ear measurement testing ensures your hearing aids are delivering the correct amplification levels for your ears. A small microphone is placed in the ear canal to verify the device’s performance in real-time.

Why Real Ear Measurement Matters:

This precise testing helps ensure your hearing aids are accurately programmed to provide the best speech clarity and sound quality possible.

Hearing Aid Repairs

Hearing aids can experience wear and tear over time. At Campbell Hearing Solutions, we offer:

  • Repairs for most major hearing aid brands
  • Warranty service and support
  • Troubleshooting and minor fixes
  • Professional cleaning and maintenance

 

We also repair devices not originally purchased from our clinic. For more complex repairs, we may coordinate with the manufacturer.

Follow-Up and Maintenance

Taking care of your hearing aids helps them last longer and perform better. Here are a few tips:

  • Keep devices dry and clean
  • Store them in a protective case when not in use
  • Replace or charge batteries as needed
  • Schedule regular maintenance visits with our team in Granger, IN

Campbell Hearing Solutions Hearing Aid Services

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, we are committed to helping you hear your best. From selecting the right hearing aid to ongoing care and adjustments, we provide expert support every step of the way.

Contact us today to schedule a consultation with the best audiologists in Granger, IN.

Here’s to taking the first step toward better hearing!

Make an Appointment

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, our dedicated team of audiologists brings decades of experience in helping people hear and working with the latest hearing technology.

We go beyond a simple hearing test—our comprehensive evaluations assess hearing, cognition, vision, balance, and dexterity, ensuring a holistic approach to your care.

Let us help you get back to doing what you love with confidence.

Home › Hearing Aids › Hearing Aids for Tinnitus Relief

Hearing Aids for Tinnitus Relief

  • David A. Campbell, M.D
  • April 19, 2025

If you hear a ringing, buzzing, or hissing sound in your ears that no one else can hear, you might be experiencing tinnitus. For many people, this phantom sound can be a constant distraction, interfering with concentration, communication, and sleep. 

Tinnitus can have many causes, like loud noise exposure, hearing loss, or medical conditions, but it’s frequently linked to hearing damage.

Fortunately, tinnitus doesn’t have to control your life. One of the most effective ways to manage it is with hearing aids. These small but powerful devices do more than amplify sound—they can also reduce how noticeable your tinnitus is. By improving your hearing and refocusing your attention, hearing aids can ease the burden of ringing in your ears.

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, we know how challenging tinnitus can be. That’s why we offer hearing aids with tinnitus support features, paired with expert guidance and care. 

If you’re tired of living with the noise, hearing aids might be the relief you need.

We provide hearing aids for tinnitus in Granger, IN.

Why Choose Hearing Aids for Tinnitus?

Hearing aids offer several advantages for people with tinnitus:

  • Tinnitus Masking – Many models offer sound therapy features like white noise or gentle tones to cover up the ringing.
  • Auditory Enrichment – Amplifying real-world sounds helps drown out internal noise.
  • Improved Focus and Sleep – Less awareness of tinnitus leads to better rest and mental clarity.
  • Reduced Stress – Lower anxiety levels can make tinnitus feel less intense.
  • Dual Benefit – Hearing aids treat both hearing loss and tinnitus simultaneously.

Masking and Attentional Effects

Built-in sound masking features help reduce the contrast between tinnitus and quiet surroundings. These soothing sounds shift your brain’s focus away from the phantom ringing and toward the sounds around you.

Auditory Stimulation

Hearing aids keep your brain stimulated with consistent sound input. This helps retrain your auditory system and reduce the brain’s habit of “filling the silence” with tinnitus.

Improved Communication

Tinnitus can make it harder to follow conversations, especially if you also have hearing loss. Hearing aids enhance speech clarity, helping you engage more confidently in social settings and enjoy daily interactions.

Working with an audiologist is essential to address tinnitus effectively. At Campbell Hearing Solutions, we provide individualized tinnitus care, hearing aid fittings, and patient counseling to help you manage your symptoms with confidence.

The Link Between Hearing Loss and Ringing in the Ears

Hearing loss and tinnitus are often connected. When your brain receives less sound input due to damaged hearing, it sometimes creates its own sound, leading to the sensation of ringing or buzzing.

In fact, around 80% of people with tinnitus also have some form of hearing loss. That’s why hearing aids are so effective—they amplify external sounds, reduce the brain’s overcompensation, and shift your attention away from the tinnitus.

Hearing aids can also have emotional benefits. Less frustration, fewer misunderstandings, and better sleep all contribute to improved mental well-being. That’s why treating hearing loss is often the first step toward relief from tinnitus.

Campbell Hearing Solutions provide expert audiological care in Ganger, IN.

Selecting the Best Hearing Aids for Tinnitus

Not all hearing aids are the same. The best device for you will depend on your hearing loss, lifestyle, and the severity of your tinnitus. Many modern hearing aids include advanced tinnitus support features, such as:

  • Sound therapy and masking sounds
  • Bluetooth streaming for calming audio
  • Personalized sound profiles
  • Adjustable programs for changing environments

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, our team will help you choose the right device and tailor it to your needs. We work with leading brands like Starkey, ReSound, Oticon, and Phonak, known for their reliable tinnitus support features.

Hearing Aids for Tinnitus in Granger, IN

You don’t have to let tinnitus disrupt your life. At Campbell Hearing Solutions, we offer hearing aids with cutting-edge tinnitus support and expert audiology care to help you find lasting relief.

Whether you’re just beginning to explore your options or you’re ready to take the next step, our team is here to guide you in selecting hearing aids in Granger, IN. 

Schedule your consultation today and discover how hearing aids can reduce your tinnitus and improve your quality of life.

Make an Appointment

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, our dedicated team of audiologists brings decades of experience in helping people hear and working with the latest hearing technology.

We go beyond a simple hearing test—our comprehensive evaluations assess hearing, cognition, vision, balance, and dexterity, ensuring a holistic approach to your care.

Let us help you get back to doing what you love with confidence.
