bodyHtml:
Skip to content

Home › Hearing Aids & Products

Hearing Aids & Products

Campbell Hearing Solutions provides quality hearing aids and products in Granger, IN, and nearby locations.

Hearing Aid Brands/Manufacturers

Campbell Hearing Solutions provides and services most major hearing aid brands. Whether you already wear hearing aids or are looking for a new device, our audiologists can help with programming, adjustments, cleaning, and repairs.

We work with a wide range of trusted brands to make sure you get the best hearing care possible.

Phonak Hearing Aids

Phonak is one of the most trusted hearing aid brands in the world. The company has been helping people hear better for over 75 years. Phonak is known for making smart, easy-to-use hearing aids with helpful features to make hearing easier for people of all ages.

Oticon Hearing Aids

Oticon has been helping people hear better since 1904. Their hearing aids use BrainHearing™ technology to support the way your brain naturally understands sound. Popular models include Oticon Real and Oticon More, which offer excellent sound clarity and noise reduction.

Widex Hearing Aids

Widex has been making hearing aids since 1956. Their Widex Moment and Widex SmartRIC hearing aids reduce sound delay, so voices and noises sound more real and clear. Widex also offers options for tinnitus relief.

Starkey Hearing Aids

Starkey hearing aids offer features like fall detection, health tracking, and tinnitus relief. Popular models include Starkey Genesis AI and Evolv AI, which are smart and easy to use with your smartphone.

Signia Hearing Aids

Signia hearing aids are known for advanced sound features and stylish designs. They offer rechargeable batteries, Bluetooth, and clear speech in noisy areas. Signia also has discreet options that are almost invisible in the ear.

FAQs:

What is the most advanced hearing aid?

Many hearing aids today come with advanced features like clear speech enhancement, Bluetooth connection, and rechargeable batteries. Different brands offer different benefits. The best hearing aid for you depends on your hearing needs and lifestyle. An audiologist can help guide you toward the most advanced option that’s right for you.

Which brand of hearing aid is best for tinnitus?

Several hearing aid brands include features to help with tinnitus, such as sound therapy or masking sounds. But what works well for one person might not be the best fit for another. The best way to find relief is to speak with an audiologist. They can recommend hearing aids and treatment options based on your symptoms.

Which hearing aid is best for severe loss?

There are many powerful hearing aids designed for people with severe hearing loss. These models offer strong amplification and clear sound. But every person’s hearing is different. The best hearing aid for you depends on your specific hearing test results. Your audiologist will help you find the right solution that meets your needs.

Custom Hearing Protection

Custom earplugs are made to fit your ears exactly. They offer better comfort and sound protection than regular earplugs. These can be used for loud work areas, concerts, shooting sports, or swimming. Custom ear protection helps protect your hearing without blocking out all sound. Talk to our audiologists about what type of ear protection is best for your needs.

Custom Ear Molds

Custom ear molds are soft molds made to fit the shape of your ear. They are most often used for custom hearing aids. These molds give a better fit and help keep sound from leaking out. Ear molds can also be used for earphones, hearing protection, or swim plugs. An audiologist takes a quick ear impression, then sends it to a lab to create a mold that fits you perfectly. This helps make sure your hearing device is both comfortable and effective.

Custom Ear Molds for Musicians

Musicians need to protect their hearing while still hearing music clearly. Custom ear molds can be used to create musician earplugs or in-ear monitors. These help block out harmful noise while keeping sound quality clear. Custom molds also give a comfortable, secure fit during performances or rehearsals.

FAQs:

Is it OK to sleep with ear plugs in?

Yes, you can sleep with earplugs, but make sure they fit well and keep your ears clean to avoid irritation or infection.

How long do ear molds last?

Custom ear molds usually last 2-5 years, depending on how often you use them and how well you care for them.

Why are ear molds used in hearing aid fitting?

Ear molds give a better fit and sound quality. They also help keep the hearing aid in place and reduce feedback or whistling.

Assistive Listening Devices

Assistive Listening Devices help people hear better in certain situations, like watching TV, talking on the phone, or being in a meeting or classroom. ALDs can work with or without hearing aids. Some common types include TV streamers, FM systems, personal amplifiers, and Bluetooth microphones.

Working with an audiologist can help you choose the right devices for your lifestyle. For example, if you often have trouble hearing in meetings or at church, an FM or loop system might help. If you have hearing aids, many ALDs can connect to them wirelessly for better sound.

Assistive devices can make a big difference in your daily life-helping you feel more connected and confident.

 

FAQs:

What is the most commonly used assistive listening device?

Personal amplifiers and TV streamers are very common. They help people hear better in noisy or distant situations.

Can you turn an iPhone into a listening device?

Yes, iPhones have a feature called Live Listen that can send sound from your phone to your hearing aids or headphones.

Where are assistive listening devices required?

Many public places, like theaters, churches, and government buildings, must provide assistive listening systems by law so people with hearing loss can hear clearly.

Make an Appointment

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, our dedicated team of audiologists brings decades of experience in helping people hear and working with the latest hearing technology.

We go beyond a simple hearing test—our comprehensive evaluations assess hearing, cognition, vision, balance, and dexterity, ensuring a holistic approach to your care.

Let us help you get back to doing what you love with confidence.

Home › Hearing Tests in Granger, IN

Hearing Tests in Granger, IN

Campbell Hearing Solutions provides comprehensive hearing tests in Granger, IN. 

A hearing test helps determine how well you hear different sounds. At Campbell Hearing Solutions, we use advanced tests to identify hearing issues and find the best solutions. Our expert audiologists in Granger, IN are here to support your hearing health.

Why Seeing a Doctor of Audiology is Important

A Doctor of Audiology is a healthcare professional trained in diagnosing and treating hearing and balance disorders. They can evaluate your hearing, explain your test results, and recommend personalized treatment options that best fit your needs.

Why Would I Need a Hearing Test?

Hearing loss often develops slowly, making it hard to notice at first. Some common signs include:

  • Difficulty hearing in noisy environments
  • Frequently asking others to repeat themselves
  • Turning up the volume on the TV or phone
  • Ringing or buzzing sounds in the ears (tinnitus)

 

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, we offer a full range of hearing tests, including:

  • Pure-tone audiometry – Measures your ability to hear different tones and pitches.
  • Speech testing – Assesses how clearly you can understand spoken words.
  • Tympanometry – Evaluates how your eardrum responds to sound and pressure.

What Are the Types of Hearing Tests?

  • Pure-Tone Audiometry – Identifies the softest sounds you can hear at different frequencies.
  • Speech Audiometry – Tests how well you recognize and understand speech at different volume levels.
  • Tympanometry – Checks for issues in the middle ear, such as fluid buildup or eardrum movement problems.

Three Main Types of Hearing Loss

  • Sensorineural Hearing Loss – Caused by damage to the inner ear or auditory nerve. This type of hearing loss is usually permanent and is often treated with hearing aids or cochlear implants.
  • Conductive Hearing Loss – Occurs when sound cannot travel through the outer or middle ear. It may be caused by ear infections, wax buildup, or other blockages and can often be treated medically or surgically.
  • Mixed Hearing Loss – A combination of sensorineural and conductive hearing loss, which may require both medical treatment and hearing technology.

Hearing Test: What to Expect

During your hearing test, you’ll wear headphones and listen to a series of tones at different volumes and pitches. The audiologist will also examine your ears and ask about your hearing history. The test is painless and typically takes 30 to 45 minutes.

Hearing Test Results

After the evaluation, the audiologist will go over your results using an audiogram, a chart that shows how well you hear across different frequencies. If hearing loss is found, they’ll discuss your treatment options, which may include hearing aids, medical follow-up, or other hearing care solutions.

Hearing Test FAQs

Can I test my own hearing?

You can try an online hearing screening, but it won’t be as detailed or accurate as a professional hearing test. Our clinic offers complete hearing evaluations for more precise results.

Will I need to do anything to prepare for a hearing test?

No special preparation is needed, but try to avoid loud noise for several hours before your appointment.

Are there any risks to hearing tests?

No—hearing tests are completely safe and painless.

How reliable are online hearing tests?

Online tests may provide a general idea of your hearing, but they cannot detect all types of hearing loss or measure hearing accurately. For the most reliable results, schedule a professional evaluation.

Can you do a hearing test on your phone?

Some phone apps offer basic hearing checks, but they don’t replace a full exam by an audiologist.

How often should I take a hearing test?

If your hearing is normal, testing every 3 to 5 years is recommended. If you have hearing loss, use hearing aids, or work in loud environments, it’s best to have your hearing checked once a year.

Hearing Tests in Granger, IN

Your hearing is an essential part of your quality of life. Audiologists at Campbell Hearing Solutions are experts in providing comprehensive hearing tests in Granger, IN.

Schedule your hearing test today and take the first step toward better hearing!

Make an Appointment

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, our dedicated team of audiologists brings decades of experience in helping people hear and working with the latest hearing technology.

We go beyond a simple hearing test—our comprehensive evaluations assess hearing, cognition, vision, balance, and dexterity, ensuring a holistic approach to your care.

Let us help you get back to doing what you love with confidence.

Home › Hearing Aids › How to Service Hearing Aids: A Simple Guide to Better Hearing and Longer Device Life

How to Service Hearing Aids: A Simple Guide to Better Hearing and Longer Device Life

  • David A. Campbell, M.D
  • February 11, 2026
  • Regular hearing aid service keeps sound clear and helps devices last longer.
  • Most hearing aid problems are caused by wax, moisture, or poor cleaning.
  • Professional service at Campbell Hearing Solutions can prevent costly hearing aid repairs.

Hearing aids work hard every day. They sit in the ear, handle sound, and deal with moisture, earwax, and dirt. Knowing how to service hearing aids is important if you want them to work well and last as long as possible. When hearing aids are not cleaned or checked often, they can stop working, sound weak, or break down sooner than expected.

This guide explains how to service hearing aids at home, when to seek professional help, and how Campbell Hearing Solutions in Granger, Indiana can support your hearing health.

Why Hearing Aid Service Matters

Hearing aids are small devices, but they are powerful tools. They help you hear speech, stay connected to loved ones, and feel safer in daily life.

Without proper service:

  • Sound quality can drop
  • Batteries may drain faster
  • Moisture can damage parts
  • Earwax can block sound

Regular service protects your investment and your hearing.

Common Problems Caused by Poor Hearing Aid Care

Many hearing aid issues are not caused by the device itself. They are caused by buildup or damage over time.

Common problems include:

  • No sound or weak sound
  • Whistling or feedback
  • Devices cutting in and out
  • Short battery life
  • Pain or itching in the ear

Most of these problems can be avoided with basic care and routine service.

Daily Hearing Aid Care: What You Can Do at Home

Daily care is the first step in learning how to service hearing aids properly.

Wipe Hearing Aids Every Night

Use a soft, dry cloth to wipe:

  • The outside of the hearing aid
  • The ear mold or dome

This removes sweat, wax, and dirt before they build up.

Open the Battery Door or Power Off Devices

At night:

  • Turn off your hearing aids
  • Open the battery door (if applicable)

This lets moisture escape and saves battery power.

Store Hearing Aids in a Safe Place

Keep hearing aids:

  • In a dry case
  • Away from pets and children
  • Off bathroom counters

Moisture and accidents are common causes of damage.

Weekly Hearing Aid Service Tasks

Weekly care goes a step further and keeps sound clear.

Check for Earwax Buildup

Look at:

  • The speaker or receiver
  • The wax guard
  • The ear mold opening

Wax is one of the most common causes of hearing aid failure.

Clean Ear Molds or Domes

If your hearing aids have removable ear molds or domes:

  • Remove them carefully
  • Wash with warm water and mild soap
  • Let them dry fully before reattaching

Never wash the hearing aid itself.

Replace Wax Guards When Needed

Wax guards protect the speaker from earwax.

Signs you need a new wax guard:

  • Sound is weak
  • Hearing aid seems blocked
  • Cleaning does not help

Wax guards are simple to replace but easy to forget.

Monthly Hearing Aid Service Tips

Once a month, take time to fully inspect your devices.

Check for Cracks or Damage

Look for:

  • Cracks in the shell
  • Loose parts
  • Bent battery doors

If you see damage, stop using the device and seek help.

Test Sound Quality

Ask yourself:

  • Does speech sound clear?
  • Is volume steady?
  • Are both hearing aids working the same?

Changes may signal a need for professional service.

Moisture: A Major Enemy of Hearing Aids

Hearing aids and moisture do not mix.

Moisture comes from:

  • Sweat
  • Humidity
  • Rain
  • Bathrooms

Even small amounts can damage internal parts.

Use a Drying System

A hearing aid dryer or dehumidifier:

  • Pulls moisture out overnight
  • Extends device life
  • Reduces repair needs

This is especially helpful in warmer months.

How to Service Rechargeable Hearing Aids

Rechargeable hearing aids need special care.

Tips include:

  • Charge every night
  • Keep charging contacts clean
  • Avoid extreme heat
  • Do not overcharge with non-approved chargers

If charging seems slow or inconsistent, service may be needed. Campbell Hearing Solutions connects you to expert audiologists in Granger, IN.

How to Service Hearing Aids With Batteries

If your hearing aids use disposable batteries:

  • Keep batteries clean and dry
  • Remove dead batteries right away
  • Store batteries at room temperature

Battery issues often look like hearing aid problems.

When Home Care Is Not Enough

Even with good daily care, hearing aids still need professional service.

You should seek help if:

  • Sound does not improve after cleaning
  • Devices stop working
  • Hearing aids cause discomfort
  • Feedback continues

Trying to fix internal parts yourself can cause more damage.

Professional Hearing Aid Service: What to Expect

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, professional hearing aid service includes:

  • Deep cleaning
  • Listening checks
  • Part replacement if needed
  • Software adjustments
  • Fit and comfort checks

This level of service cannot be done at home.

How Often Should Hearing Aids Be Professionally Serviced?

Most patients benefit from:

  • Professional cleaning every 6 months
  • Hearing checks once a year
  • Immediate service when problems arise

Hearing needs can change over time.

Why Regular Service Improves Hearing Results

Serviced hearing aids:

  • Sound clearer
  • Work more reliably
  • Last longer
  • Support better hearing outcomes

Clear sound helps the brain stay active and engaged.

Hearing Aid Service and Ear Health

Clean hearing aids support healthy ears.

Poorly maintained devices can cause:

  • Ear infections
  • Skin irritation
  • Itching or redness

Professional service includes checking ear health, not just devices.

Hearing Aid Service for Seniors

Older adults may need more frequent service because:

  • Skin produces more wax
  • Vision and hand strength may change
  • Devices may be worn longer each day

Local support makes care easier and safer.

Hearing Aid Service for Active Adults

If you are active or outdoors often:

  • Sweat and dirt increase buildup
  • Moisture risk is higher

Regular service helps keep devices working in all conditions.

Signs Your Hearing Aids Need Immediate Service

Call for help if:

  • Hearing aid stops working suddenly
  • There is pain or drainage from the ear
  • Sound cuts in and out
  • Battery drains too fast

Quick service prevents bigger problems.

Why Choose Campbell Hearing Solutions in Granger, IN

Campbell Hearing Solutions provides:

  • Local, personalized care
  • Professional hearing aid service
  • Clear instructions and support
  • Long-term hearing health planning

We understand how important your hearing is to daily life.

Tips to Make Hearing Aids Last Longer

Simple habits make a big difference:

  • Clean daily
  • Store properly
  • Avoid moisture
  • Schedule regular service

Good care saves money and frustration.

Hearing Aid Service Is Part of Hearing Health

Servicing hearing aids is not just about devices. It supports:

  • Communication
  • Safety
  • Confidence
  • Quality of life

Well-serviced hearing aids help you stay connected.

How to Service Hearing Aids the Right Way

Knowing how to service hearing aids helps protect your hearing, your comfort, and your investment. Daily cleaning, weekly checks, and regular professional service all play a role. When problems come up, local care matters.

At Campbell Hearing Solutions in Granger, Indiana, we are here to help you keep your hearing aids working their best. If your devices need service or you have questions about how to service hearing aids, our team is ready to support you.

Contact us today to schedule an appointment!

Make an Appointment

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, our dedicated team of audiologists brings decades of experience in helping people hear and working with the latest hearing technology.

We go beyond a simple hearing test—our comprehensive evaluations assess hearing, cognition, vision, balance, and dexterity, ensuring a holistic approach to your care.

Let us help you get back to doing what you love with confidence.

Home › Hearing Aids › Hyperacusis: Understanding and Managing Sound Sensitivity

Hyperacusis: Understanding and Managing Sound Sensitivity

  • David A. Campbell, M.D
  • April 12, 2025

Does everyday sound feel overwhelming to you? If normal conversations, household noises, or the hum of your car seem unusually loud or even painful, you might be experiencing a condition called hyperacusis. This hearing disorder makes ordinary sounds feel unbearable and can significantly impact daily life. 

At Campbell Hearing Solutions in Granger, IN, we understand how challenging sound sensitivity can be. That’s why we’re here to help you understand hyperacusis, its causes, and how you can find relief through expert care and compassionate support. We provide hearing tests and other diagnostics so that you can get the hearing solution for your specific hearing concerns.

Hyperacusis: Signs and Symptoms

Hyperacusis is more than just a dislike for loud noises. It’s a heightened sensitivity that can make even moderate sounds seem painfully loud. People with hyperacusis often describe everyday noises like clinking dishes, car engines, or a baby crying as physically painful or distressing. This condition may be accompanied by anxiety, irritability, or even social withdrawal due to the discomfort it causes.

Common signs include:

  • Discomfort or pain when hearing normal sounds
  • Increased sensitivity to high-pitched or sudden noises
  • Avoidance of noisy environments
  • Trouble concentrating due to sound distractions
  • Ear fullness or ringing (tinnitus)

If you’re looking for expert audiologists in Granger, IN who can help you with your hearing, please give Campbell Hearing Solutions a call. Our audiologists can help assess your hearing and find a treatment plan tailored to your needs.

Hyperacusis: Causes

There are several potential causes of hyperacusis. Often, it results from damage to the inner ear or hearing system due to:

  • Noise exposure (loud music, machinery, or explosions)
  • Head injuries or concussions
  • Ear infections or ear surgeries
  • Neurological conditions like migraines or Lyme disease
  • Medication side effects
  • Chronic stress or anxiety

In some cases, hyperacusis may develop gradually, while in others it appears suddenly. Regardless of how it starts, the impact on daily life can be frustrating and emotionally draining. Identifying the root cause is the first step toward managing your symptoms and reclaiming comfort in your everyday environment.

Diagnosing Hyperacusis

Getting the right diagnosis is essential for proper treatment. At Campbell Hearing Solutions our experienced Granger, IN audiologists use specialized hearing tests to measure your sound sensitivity and pinpoint your triggers. These assessments help us distinguish hyperacusis from other hearing conditions like tinnitus or hearing loss.

Don’t wait to seek help, hyperacusis can worsen without proper care. We provide hearing diagnostic services in Granger, IN.

Hyperacusis Treatment

Treating hyperacusis often involves a combination of sound therapy, counseling, and lifestyle changes. One common approach is desensitization therapy, where the ears are gradually retrained to tolerate normal sound levels. This can be done using low-level noise generators or environmental sounds. Cognitive behavioral therapy (CBT) is also helpful for managing the emotional response to sound sensitivity.

In some cases, hearing aids with sound-masking features can provide relief, especially if tinnitus is also present. At Campbell Hearing Solutions, we offer customized treatment plans based on your symptoms, triggers, and lifestyle to help you regain control and comfort. We provide a wide selection of hearing solutions and hearing aids in Granger, IN.

Hyperacusis Home Remedies

While professional treatment is always recommended, there are home remedies and lifestyle changes that may help reduce the impact of hyperacusis:

  • Create a calming environment: Avoid overly quiet spaces, which can make sound sensitivity worse. Gentle background noise (like soft music or a white noise machine) can help ease the discomfort.
  • Wear sound-filtering earplugs: If you’re in a noisy area, these earplugs can reduce intensity without blocking sound completely. Avoid using foam earplugs all the time, as overprotection can make hyperacusis worse.
  • Practice relaxation techniques: Stress and anxiety can heighten sensitivity. Try mindfulness, deep breathing, or meditation to calm your nervous system.
  • Limit loud sound exposure: Avoid concerts, loud machinery, or other high-volume environments, especially without hearing protection.
  • Keep a sound journal: Track which sounds trigger discomfort and share this information with your audiologist.

Remember, while these methods may provide temporary relief, they do not replace a full evaluation by a qualified professional. 

Hyperacusis Risk Factors

Anyone can develop hyperacusis, but certain risk factors increase the likelihood:

  • Frequent exposure to loud noise, such as musicians or construction workers
  • A history of ear infections or trauma
  • Neurological conditions, including migraines or epilepsy
  • Mental health conditions, such as anxiety or PTSD
  • Use of ototoxic medications that can damage hearing structures
  • Tinnitus or hearing loss may also be linked to hyperacusis

Understanding these risk factors can help with early detection and management. If you fall into one of these categories and notice increased sound sensitivity, it’s best to schedule a hearing evaluation with an audiologist.

Hearing Tests in Granger, IN

Hyperacusis can make even the simplest sounds feel overwhelming, but you don’t have to face it alone. With the right diagnosis and support, it’s possible to reduce your sensitivity and improve your quality of life. 

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, we are committed to providing compassionate care and personalized treatment for every patient. If you or a loved one is experiencing sound sensitivity, contact us today to schedule a consultation. 

We provide comprehensive hearing tests in Granger, IN.

Let us help you find comfort in sound again!

We go beyond a simple hearing test—our comprehensive evaluations assess hearing, cognition, vision, balance, and dexterity, ensuring a holistic approach to your care.

Insurance and Billing

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, we want to make your care experience as smooth and transparent as possible. Whether you’re using insurance or paying out-of-pocket, we are committed to helping you understand your rights, your protections, and your options when it comes to medical billing.

Insurance Information

We accept a variety of insurance plans. 

Before your visit, we recommend checking with your insurance provider to confirm coverage for hearing evaluations, hearing aids, and other services.

If you’re not sure whether your insurance covers our services, our team is happy to help guide you through the process. 

Please call our office and we’ll work with you to verify your benefits and answer your questions.

Have Questions?

Campbell Hearing Solutions is here to answer your questions about billing, estimates, or insurance coverage. 

Please don’t hesitate to contact our friendly staff and audiologists in Granger, IN!

Make an Appointment

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, our dedicated team of audiologists brings decades of experience in helping people hear and working with the latest hearing technology.

We go beyond a simple hearing test—our comprehensive evaluations assess hearing, cognition, vision, balance, and dexterity, ensuring a holistic approach to your care.

Let us help you get back to doing what you love with confidence.

Skip to content

Home › Lyric Hearing Aids

Lyric Hearing Aids

Campbell Hearing Solutions is an authorized provider of Lyric hearing aids in Granger, IN.

Are you looking for hearing loss solutions other than traditional hearing aids? Lyric hearing aids may be a worthy device to check. Lyric comes in a very discreet design and does not need to be removed daily.

What Makes Lyric Different?

Lyric hearing aids are unlike anything else on the market. These ultra-small devices are inserted deep into the ear canal by one of our trained professionals and remain there for weeks at a time. You don’t take them out to sleep, shower, or exercise. They stay in place 24/7, delivering natural, clear sound by using your ear’s natural anatomy to direct sound to your eardrum.

No daily maintenance. No visible parts. Just better hearing with zero hassle.

Why Patients Choose Lyric

Many of our patients in Granger and surrounding communities choose Lyric because they:

  • Want a completely invisible hearing aid
  • Prefer a “set-it-and-forget-it” experience
  • Struggle with changing tiny batteries or managing settings
  • Want the freedom to wear their hearing aid while showering, sleeping, or working out

 

Lyric hearing aids offer a discreet yet effective hearing solution for people diagnosed with mild to moderate hearing loss.

Subscription-Based, Personalized Hearing Care

Lyric is offered on a subscription basis. This means that you don’t simply purchase the device.

A Lyric provider will insert a new Lyric device at regular intervals, ensuring it stays comfortable and effective.

When you avail a Lyric subscription, this includes device fittings, replacements, updates, and ongoing care.

Lyric may be ideal if you:

  • Have mild to moderate hearing loss
  • Have normal-sized ear canals
  • Want a discreet, low-maintenance option
  • Value natural sound quality and comfort

 

However, if you have narrow ear canals, certain skin sensitivities, or more complex hearing needs, we’ll help you explore other options that may be a better fit.

Comfortable, Invisible, Worry-Free Hearing

The biggest advantage of Lyric is how effortless it is. You can wear headphones, go about your daily routine, and even sleep on your side without removing your hearing aid.

It’s a simple solution that makes a big difference in how you feel about managing hearing loss.

Lyric Hearing Aids in Granger, IN

If you’re curious to learn more about Lyric hearing aids, the best first step is to come in and talk with an authorized Lyric provider.

Campbell Hearing Solutions connects you to Lyric providers in Granger, IN.

Call us today to schedule your appointment. We look forward to seeing you!

We go beyond a simple hearing test—our comprehensive evaluations assess hearing, cognition, vision, balance, and dexterity, ensuring a holistic approach to your care.

Let us help you get back to doing what you love with confidence.

Skip to content

Home › Hearing Aids › How Accurate Are Online Hearing Tests? What to Know Before You Try One

How Accurate Are Online Hearing Tests? What to Know Before You Try One

  • David A. Campbell, M.D
  • November 26, 2025

Many people use an online hearing test to check their hearing at home. These quick and easy tools can give a general idea of hearing ability, but they are not a replacement for a professional exam. At Campbell Hearing Solutions, we explain how online hearing tests work, their limits, and why visiting an audiologist provides the most reliable answers about your hearing health. We provide hearing tests in Granger, IN.

Understanding Online Hearing Tests 

If you’ve noticed people mumbling more often or have been told to turn down the volume when it sounds fine to you, you might be tempted to try a free online hearing test. These digital tools are fast and convenient, often taking less than 10 minutes to complete. They play tones or words through your headphones and ask you to respond when you hear something. 

While these tests can raise awareness of possible hearing issues, they cannot replace a professional hearing evaluation. 

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, we encourage you to use online tests as a helpful starting point, not as a diagnosis. We provide comprehensive hearing tests in Granger, IN.

How Online Hearing Tests Work 

Online hearing tests aim to simulate what happens during a basic hearing screening. Typically, you’ll: 

  1. Use your computer, tablet, or phone with headphones or speakers. 
  2. Listen to tones or speech patterns at different volumes and pitches. 
  3. Indicate when you can or can’t hear certain sounds. 
  4. The test then provides a summary of your hearing ability, often showing whether your results fall within a “normal,” “mild,” or “moderate” range of hearing loss. 

While this may seem like a convenient method to check your hearing, the results are limited by several factors, including your device quality, background noise, and the test’s accuracy. 

How Accurate Are Online Hearing Tests?

Online hearing tests can give a general idea of your hearing ability, but they are far from perfect. 

  • Their accuracy depends heavily on: 
  • The type and quality of your headphones or speakers 
  • Your computer or phone’s volume settings 
  • The quietness of your environment 

Even small changes, like background noise or low-quality audio, can affect your results. 

The test might falsely suggest you have hearing loss, or it could miss a real problem entirely. Online hearing tests cannot detect the underlying causes of hearing changes. 

Professional audiologists can identify whether hearing loss comes from: 

  • Earwax blockage 
  • Fluid or infection in the ear 
  • Middle ear pressure problems 
  • Nerve damage or inner ear disorders 

An audiologist’s expertise allows them to find the root cause, something an online tool simply cannot do. 

Do Online Hearing Tests Work? 

The short answer is yes, online hearing tests work as a first step, but not as a complete solution. They can help raise awareness and encourage people to seek professional care if something seems off. 

For example, if your online results suggest mild hearing loss, it’s a sign to schedule a full exam with an audiologist. However, if you rely only on an online test, you risk missing more serious issues that require treatment. 

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, we often see patients who took an online test and later discovered their hearing issues were caused by something simple, like earwax buildup, or something more serious, such as an ear infection or nerve damage. 

An online test can point you in the right direction, but only a professional can give you accurate, complete answers. 

Campbell Hearing Solutions connects you to an expert audiologist in Granger, IN.

The Limits of Online Hearing Tests 

Here’s what online hearing tests can’t do: 

They’re not reliable for diagnosis. 

Results can vary based on your headphones, device, and surroundings. 

They can’t detect earwax, fluid, or infection. 

These treatable conditions often cause hearing problems. 

They don’t assess overall ear health. 

Professional exams measure eardrum movement and middle ear function. 

They miss other hearing factors. 

Conditions like tinnitus, medication side effects, or neurological issues go unnoticed. 

They can delay care. 

If you rely on an online result and skip seeing a professional, you could miss early treatment opportunities. 

Think of an online hearing test as a quick check, not a full evaluation. It’s helpful for awareness, but it should never be your only step. 

Why a Professional Hearing Test Is Better 

When you visit Campbell Hearing Solutions, you’ll receive a complete hearing evaluation-not just a quick tone test. Our audiologists use advanced tools and expertise to measure how well you hear in different environments and identify what’s causing your hearing challenges. 

A professional hearing exam includes: 

A full ear exam: 

Checking for earwax, infection, or damage. 

Precise testing: 

Measuring hearing across various pitches and speech sounds in a quiet, controlled room. 

Middle ear analysis: 

Assessing eardrum movement and ear pressure. 

Speech clarity testing: 

Understanding how well you hear and process words in noise. 

Personalized discussion: 

Reviewing your results and creating a hearing care plan that fits your lifestyle. This level of detail simply isn’t possible with an online hearing test. 

When to Schedule a Hearing Evaluation 

If you notice any of the following signs, it’s time to book a professional hearing test: 

  • People seem to mumble often. 
  • You ask others to repeat themselves. 
  • You struggle to follow conversations in noisy settings. 
  • You turn up the TV or radio louder than others prefer. 
  • You hear ringing or buzzing sounds in your ears (tinnitus). 

Even if your online test results say your hearing is fine, these symptoms should not be ignored. Hearing loss can develop gradually, and early detection makes treatment more effective. 

The Benefits of Visiting an Audiologist 

A licensed audiologist does more than test your hearing-they evaluate your entire auditory system. At Campbell Hearing Solutions, we provide personalized care tailored to your specific needs and results. 

Our audiologists will: 

  • Explain your results clearly 
  • Identify the cause of your hearing loss 
  • Recommend hearing aids, treatment, or other hearing solutions if needed 
  • Offer ongoing care to monitor your hearing health over time 

We’re dedicated to helping you maintain healthy hearing and stay connected to the world around you. 

Comprehensive Hearing Tests in Granger, IN 

Online hearing tests can be a useful starting point, but they are not a substitute for professional care. If you’re serious about your hearing health, the best way to understand your hearing is with a comprehensive hearing test from an experienced audiologist. 

Here at Campbell Hearing Solutions, we use advanced technology and personalized care to evaluate your hearing accurately and safely. Whether you’ve taken an online test or simply suspect a change in your hearing, we’re here to help. 

Schedule your professional hearing test in Granger, IN today and take the next step toward better hearing and a better quality of life. 

An online hearing test might give you a glimpse of your hearing health, but a visit with an audiologist will give you the full picture-and the solutions you deserve.

Make an Appointment

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, our dedicated team of audiologists brings decades of experience in helping people hear and working with the latest hearing technology.

We go beyond a simple hearing test—our comprehensive evaluations assess hearing, cognition, vision, balance, and dexterity, ensuring a holistic approach to your care.

Let us help you get back to doing what you love with confidence.

bodyHtml:
Skip to content

Home › Oticon Hearing Aids

Oticon Hearing Aids

Campbell Hearing Solutions is an authorized provider of Oticon hearing aids in Granger, IN.

Oticon is a global leader in hearing technology, committed to helping people hear clearly and communicate with confidence. With a strong focus on research and innovation, Oticon designs hearing aids that deliver natural, clear sound and an improved listening experience.

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, we share Oticon’s commitment to providing top-quality hearing care. Our goal is to help you hear your best-whether you’re at home, at work, or enjoying time with others.

Oticon Hearing Aid Models Available in Granger, IN

Oticon Zeal

Oticon Zeal is the first hearing aid designed to do it all while staying completely unseen. Built for people who want powerful technology in an ultra-discreet design, Zeal delivers exceptional sound clarity, all-day comfort, and seamless connectivity in one tiny device no one will notice.

Zeal transforms the idea of what a hearing aid should be. Its small, nearly invisible design sits comfortably in your ear, giving you the confidence to live fully without drawing attention to your device. Powered by Oticon’s advanced BrainHearing™ technology, Zeal supports the brain’s natural way of processing sound. Enjoy clear, balanced, and comfortable hearing, even in noisy places.

Unique Features:

  • Made to Connect: Stream calls, meetings, music, and more directly from your phone, Apple Watch, TV, computer, or tablet. Zeal brings your digital world to life with smooth, high-quality wireless audio.
  • Reliable Rechargeability: No disposable batteries. With fast, dependable charging, you get all-day power that keeps up with your lifestyle.
  • Same-Day Fitting: Zeal is designed for convenience, offering the option for same-day fitting so you can begin hearing better right away.

Oticon Jet PX

Oticon Jet PX is a smart hearing aid that uses advanced technology (called Deep Neural Network or DNN) to help you hear clearly. With features like Wind Noise Management and Speech Guard, it helps block out sounds that get in the way of talking to others. 

If you have ringing in your ears (tinnitus), Jet PX has built-in sounds to help you feel better. You can also connect it to your phone to listen to music, take calls, or change settings using the Oticon app. It comes in different colors and styles, and you can even get a rechargeable version.

Unique Features:

  • AI-powered OpenSound Navigator with DNN: Improves hearing with clear, natural sound.
  • Tinnitus SoundSupport: Ideal for users with tinnitus as it gives access to customizable relief sounds.
  • SuperShield: Quickly adapts and prevents feedback before it occurs.

Oticon Intent

Oticon Intent is a smart hearing aid that adapts in real-time to your environment. Using 4D Sensor Technology, it tracks your movement, speech, and surroundings to give you the best hearing experience wherever you go.

Powered by Deep Neural Network (DNN) technology, Oticon Intent mimics how the brain naturally processes sound, helping you understand speech more clearly and with less effort.

Key Features:

  • Bluetooth® LE Audio – Connects to multiple devices for direct audio streaming
  • Self-Calibrating Speaker – Adjusts sound automatically for better clarity
  • Fast Charging – Charges 33% faster for all-day use

Oticon Real

Oticon Real is designed for clear everyday listening-even in noisy settings. It reduces sudden distracting sounds like wind or hair movement. With DNN technology trained on 12 million real-life sounds, it helps you understand speech better in real life situations.

Available in miniRITE (receiver-in-the-ear) and miniBTE (behind-the-ear) styles, Oticon Real is suitable for mild to profound hearing loss and comes in both battery and rechargeable versions.

Key Features:

  • Bluetooth Streaming – Works with iPhone® and Android™ devices
  • App Control – Adjust settings with the Oticon Companion or Oticon ON app
  • Tinnitus Relief – Includes Tinnitus SoundSupport™ for soothing sound therapy

Oticon Own

Oticon Own uses smart technology to help you hear better in noisy environments like restaurants or family gatherings. It separates speech from background noise to make conversations easier to follow.

This hearing aid is custom-molded to fit your ears perfectly. Your hearing professional will take an ear mold to ensure a secure and comfortable fit.

Key Features:

  • Bluetooth Streaming – Connects to iPhone® and Android™
  • App Control – Manage settings through the Oticon apps
  • Tinnitus Relief – Includes Tinnitus SoundSupport™ for relief from ringing

Oticon Zircon

Oticon Zircon offers clear sound with balanced volume so voices don’t sound muffled or overly loud. It also provides 360-degree hearing, so you can hear sound from all around without turning your head.

Zircon includes Bluetooth for connecting to your phone, TV, and more. A rechargeable version is also available, offering convenience and all-day use.

Key Features:

  • Bluetooth Streaming – Direct connection to smart devices
  • App Control – Manage your hearing aid through the Oticon apps
  • Tinnitus Relief – Features soothing sounds through Tinnitus SoundSupport™

Oticon CROS

Oticon CROS helps individuals with single-sided deafness by transmitting sound from the weaker ear to the stronger one. It supports both CROS (no hearing in one ear) and BiCROS (some hearing in the better ear).

Oticon CROS works with many models, including:

  • Oticon Real™
  • Oticon More™
  • Oticon Zircon
  • Oticon Opn S™
  • Oticon Play PX
  • Oticon Xceed

 

Key Features:

  • TwinLink™ Bluetooth – Stream calls and music while hearing from both sides
  • App Control – Make adjustments with Oticon’s apps
  • Tinnitus Relief – Includes sound therapy for tinnitus management

Oticon Play PX

Oticon Play PX is designed for children and supports speech and language development. It is the first pediatric hearing aid with DNN technology, helping kids hear better at school and during play.

Available in miniRITE and miniBTE styles, it supports slight to severe hearing loss and comes in battery or rechargeable options.

Key Features:

  • Tamper-Resistant – Safe battery compartment for kids
  • Water-Resistant (IP68) – Protects against dust and moisture
  • LED Indicator – Lets caregivers monitor device status
  • Bluetooth Streaming – Works with smartphones and tablets

 

Additional Accessories for Children:

  • EduMic – Classroom microphone to improve teacher’s voice clarity
  • Amigo FM – Helps kids hear over distance and noise
  • ConnectClip – Streams voices and sounds from up to 65 feet away

Oticon Xceed

Oticon Xceed is built for those with severe to profound hearing loss. It delivers loud, clear, and natural sound, even in noisy environments. Xceed offers 360-degree hearing, reducing background noise so you can focus on what matters most.

Using BrainHearing™ technology, it helps your brain process sound more naturally, reducing listening fatigue. It also offers Bluetooth connectivity and a rechargeable option.

Key Features:

  • Bluetooth Streaming – Connects directly to devices
  • App Control – Easily manage settings with Oticon apps
  • Tinnitus Relief – Soothing sounds for tinnitus comfort

Assistive Listening Devices & Technology by Oticon

  • ConnectClip
  • Remote Control
  • Streamer Pro
  • TV Adapter

Older Oticon Hearing Aids We Service

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, we continue to support and repair older Oticon models, including:

  • Oticon Opn
  • Oticon Opn Play
  • Oticon Opn S
  • Oticon Ruby
  • Oticon Siya
  • Oticon More

If you have an older Oticon device, we’re here to help-just contact us for service and support.

Frequently Asked Questions About Oticon Hearing Aids

How long do Oticon hearing aids last?

Most in-the-ear hearing aids last around 5 years, and behind-the-ear styles can last 5-6 years. With proper care, they can last even longer, but many people upgrade to enjoy new technology.

What is Oticon’s newest hearing aid?

As of March 2026, Oticon’s newest and most innovative hearing aid is the Oticon Zeal.

While the Oticon Intent™ (flagship behind-the-ear model) received a significant hardware expansion in late 2025, the Zeal is the newest standalone product, officially hitting the U.S. and global markets in January 2026.

Which Oticon hearing aids help with tinnitus?

Many Oticon hearing aids include Tinnitus SoundSupport™, which offers soothing sounds to reduce tinnitus symptoms. These models include:

  • Oticon Intent
  • Oticon Real
  • Oticon Own (except CIC/IIC models)
  • Oticon Zircon
  • Oticon CROS
  • Oticon Xceed
  • Oticon Play PX (for children with tinnitus)

 

If you’re experiencing tinnitus, our Granger, IN audiologists at Campbell Hearing Solutions can help you choose the best Oticon hearing aid for your needs.

Find the Right Oticon Hearing Aid for You

Let us help you choose the best Oticon hearing aid for your needs. We are an authorized provider of Oticon hearing aids in Granger, IN.

Contact us today to learn more!

Make an Appointment

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, our dedicated team of audiologists brings decades of experience in helping people hear and working with the latest hearing technology.

We go beyond a simple hearing test—our comprehensive evaluations assess hearing, cognition, vision, balance, and dexterity, ensuring a holistic approach to your care.

Let us help you get back to doing what you love with confidence.

Home › Hearing Aids › Should You Buy Over-the-Counter Hearing Aids? A Complete Guide to Making the Right Choice

Should You Buy Over-the-Counter Hearing Aids? A Complete Guide to Making the Right Choice

  • David A. Campbell, M.D
  • December 10, 2025

Many adults wonder whether over-the-counter hearing aids are a safe and effective option, especially when they want quick help or a lower-cost device. In this guide, you’ll learn what OTC hearing aids are, who they’re meant for, and where they fall short. With clear explanations and simple language, we break down how OTC devices compare to prescription hearing aids, and why a professional hearing test still matters for long-term success.

If you’ve been having trouble hearing soft voices, following conversations in small groups, or keeping the TV volume at a normal level, you might be thinking about trying over-the-counter hearing aids. Their accessibility, availability, and lower cost make them appealing to many adults. You can walk into a store, pick a pair off the shelf, and start using them the same day. This level of convenience is one of the main reasons OTC hearing aids have become so popular.

But while OTC devices offer benefits, they also come with limitations. Not everyone understands how they work, who they’re designed for, and when professional hearing care is still necessary. At Campbell Hearing Solutions in Granger, IN, our goal is to help you make safe, confident choices about your hearing. We talk with many patients who wonder whether OTC devices are good enough or if they should schedule a hearing evaluation instead.

This guide breaks down everything you need to know before buying OTC hearing aids so you can protect your hearing and choose the option that truly meets your needs.

What Exactly Are Over-the-Counter Hearing Aids?

Over-the-counter hearing aids sold online or in retail stores are devices intended for adults aged 18 and older with mild to moderate hearing loss. Unlike prescription hearing aids, you do not need a hearing test, medical exam, or audiologist appointment to purchase them. They can be bought in:

  • Pharmacies
  • Big-box stores
  • Electronics stores
  • Online retailers

OTC hearing aids became legal after the FDA released new regulations in 2022. These rules were designed to increase accessibility and help adults get basic hearing support without the cost or time of a clinic visit.

OTC hearing aids are meant for people who:

  • Have mild hearing difficulty
  • Need louder sound in quiet or small-group settings
  • Have no ear pain, ear drainage, or dizziness
  • Do not have sudden or one-sided hearing loss
  • Are comfortable setting up the device themselves

OTC hearing aids are “one-size-fits-most,” and users are responsible for:

  • Fitting the device
  • Adjusting sound settings
  • Testing comfort
  • Managing the battery or charging system
  • Making any needed adjustments at home

For some people, this works well. For others with issues concerning their hearing and ear health, it quickly becomes frustrating.

How Do OTC Hearing Aids Differ From Prescription Hearing Aids?

It’s easy to assume OTC and professional hearing aids are the same. But in reality, they are not. The differences are important.

1. Prescription Hearing Aids Are Custom Programmed

OTC hearing aids use general sound settings.
Prescription devices are programmed to your exact hearing test results.

This includes:

  • Boosting only the frequencies where your hearing is weak
  • Softening loud sounds
  • Reducing background noise
  • Sharpening voices
  • Adjusting clarity
  • Fine-tuning your device over time

OTC hearing aids make all sounds louder. Prescription hearing aids make the right sounds clearer.

2. Technology Levels Differ Greatly

Modern prescription hearing aids offer advanced technology, such as:

  • Bluetooth streaming
  • Background noise reduction
  • Directional microphones
  • Automatic scene detection
  • Feedback control
  • Tinnitus support
  • Rechargeable batteries

OTC devices usually offer only:

  • Basic amplification
  • Simple volume control
  • Limited noise filtering

3. No Real-Ear Measurement with OTC

Audiologists use Real-Ear Measurements to verify amplification inside your ear canal. This ensures sound is safe, clear, and accurate.

OTC devices cannot be verified this way.

4. Support and Ongoing Assistance

When you purchase a device from an audiologist, you receive professional assistance, including:

  • Cleanings
  • Repairs
  • Adjustments
  • Comfort fixes
  • Follow-up visits
  • Communication coaching

OTC devices rarely include long-term support.

5. Comfort and Fit Differences

OTC hearing aids are mass-produced and may feel uncomfortable over long hours. Prescription devices can be fitted with custom molds for comfort and stability.

6. Trial Period and Return Policies

OTC hearing aids usually include a trial period, but policies vary widely. Some offer 14 days, others 30–60 days.

Prescription devices generally include a 30-day trial with support and adjustments included.

Campbell Hearing Solutions offers a wide selection of hearing aids in Granger, IN.

Who Is a Good Candidate for OTC Hearing Aids?

OTC hearing aids can be helpful for some adults, especially if you:

  • Have very mild hearing loss
  • Do not experience ringing, blockage, pain, or dizziness
  • Only need a small boost in quiet places
  • Hear well in most situations but struggle occasionally
  • Want an affordable option before exploring clinical care
  • Are you comfortable adjusting and testing the device yourself

If you fall into this category, OTC devices may offer a good starting point.

Who Should NOT Use OTC Hearing Aids?

OTC hearing aids are not recommended for adults with:

  • Moderate or severe hearing loss
  • Ringing in only one ear
  • Sudden hearing loss
  • Ear drainage or pain
  • Ear fullness or pressure
  • Dizziness or balance problems
  • History of ear surgery
  • Permanent communication difficulties
  • Difficulty hearing children or soft voices

These signs may point to medical issues that need attention—not just more volume.

Benefits of Over-the-Counter Hearing Aids

While OTC devices are not a replacement for professional care, they do offer real benefits.

1. Lower Cost

OTC hearing aids are usually between $300–$900, depending on the model and technology level.

2. Wide Availability

Because they are sold in many stores and online, availability is a major advantage.

3. More Accessibility

OTC hearing aids help people who might not be ready or able to see an audiologist right away.

4. Convenience

You can buy them right away and start using them the same day.

5. A Way to “Try Out” Amplification

For people unsure whether hearing aids will help, OTC devices may offer an entry point before moving toward custom care.

Limitations and Risks of OTC Hearing Aids

Despite benefits, OTC devices come with several important limitations.

1. Not Strong Enough for Most Users

Around 90% of people with hearing loss fall outside the “mild” range. For them, OTC devices will not help and may make the problem worse.

2. They Can Make Sound More Confusing

OTC hearing aids make all sounds louder, from background noise, clanking dishes, traffic, to voices. Many people return their devices because everything sounds “jumbled.”

3. No Professional Diagnosis

Without a hearing test, you may be missing:

  • Blocked ears
  • Middle ear fluid
  • Earwax buildup
  • Eardrum issues
  • Sensorineural hearing loss
  • Medical ear disease

OTC devices only make symptoms louder, but they do not address the cause.

4. Comfort Problems

Because OTC devices are not custom-fitted, many users report:

  • Soreness
  • Itching
  • Feedback whistling
  • Poor stability
  • Plugged-ear feeling

5. No Long-Term Adjustment

Your hearing needs may change. OTC devices cannot adjust to new levels without expert help.

OTC vs. Prescription Hearing Aids: A Simple Comparison

Should You Try OTC Hearing Aids First?

Trying OTC hearing aids may be reasonable if:

  • You want a simple starting point
  • You believe your hearing loss is mild
  • You want to test amplification without a big investment
  • You understand the limits

If you do try OTC devices, keep expectations realistic and use the trial period wisely.

Signs that OTC hearing aids are not working for you include:

  • Voices still sound unclear
  • You struggle in noisy places
  • Your ears feel plugged
  • You turn the volume too high
  • You become tired of listening
  • You still miss important sounds

If these issues happen, prescription hearing aids and a proper evaluation are the next step.

How Campbell Hearing Solutions Helps You Hear Better

At Campbell Hearing Solutions in Granger, IN, we offer:

  • Comprehensive hearing evaluations
  • Middle ear and pressure testing
  • Hearing aid fittings
  • Real-Ear Measurements
  • Tinnitus assessments
  • Ongoing adjustments
  • Repairs, cleanings, and battery support
  • Communication counseling

Our goal is to give you clear answers, personalized recommendations, and hearing support you can trust.

Final Thoughts

Buying over-the-counter hearing aids may seem like an easy choice because of their accessibility and convenience, but it’s important to understand what they can and cannot do. OTC hearing aids provide basic amplification for mild hearing loss, but they cannot replace professional testing, custom adjustment, or long-term support. For many people, working with an audiologist leads to better clarity, comfort, and confidence.

If you’re unsure which option is right for you, Campbell Hearing Solutions is here to help. Our team can test your hearing, explain your results, and guide you toward the safest and most effective solution. Whether you start with OTC devices or choose prescription hearing aids, we’ll help you take the next step toward better hearing.

If you’re ready to learn more about over-the-counter hearing aids, schedule your appointment today. 

Your hearing matters! Getting the right help can make every conversation clearer, easier, and more enjoyable.

Make an Appointment

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, our dedicated team of audiologists brings decades of experience in helping people hear and working with the latest hearing technology.

We go beyond a simple hearing test—our comprehensive evaluations assess hearing, cognition, vision, balance, and dexterity, ensuring a holistic approach to your care.

Let us help you get back to doing what you love with confidence.

Home › Patient Information | Campbell Hearing Solutions

Patient Information | Campbell Hearing Solutions

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, we’re here to make your visit as easy and comfortable as possible. 

To help us get to know you and your hearing needs, we encourage you to complete our Patient Information Form before your appointment.

You can download the form, fill it out at your convenience, and bring it with you on the day of your visit. This small step helps us prepare in advance and ensures your appointment runs smoothly, giving us more time to focus on what matters—your hearing health.

Start your path to better hearing today by completing your form!

If you have any questions or need help along the way, our friendly team is just a phone call away.

Make an Appointment

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, our dedicated team of audiologists brings decades of experience in helping people hear and working with the latest hearing technology.

We go beyond a simple hearing test—our comprehensive evaluations assess hearing, cognition, vision, balance, and dexterity, ensuring a holistic approach to your care.

Let us help you get back to doing what you love with confidence.

Book Appointment

bodyHtml:
Skip to content

Home › Phonak Hearing Aids

Phonak Hearing Aids

Campbell Hearing Solutions is an authorized provider of Phonak hearing aids in Granger, IN.

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, we believe that better hearing supports better overall well-being. Phonak shares this belief with their motto, “Well-Hearing is Well-Being.”

Hearing is about more than just sound-it’s essential for thinking clearly, managing emotions, and staying socially connected. Phonak’s advanced hearing aids are built to help people hear better, stay engaged, and enjoy life to the fullest.

Current Phonak Hearing Aid Models

Phonak Infinio Ultra Line

The Phonak Infinio Ultra Line represents the next evolution in hearing technology, delivering elevated clarity, efficiency, and adaptability through advanced AI processing. Released in October 2025, this flagship upgrade enhances the foundational Infinio platform with smarter sound recognition, better battery performance, and simplified maintenance. 

For many existing Phonak users, these improvements can be activated through an in-clinic firmware update, allowing current Infinio or Infinio Sphere devices to perform at an entirely new level without requiring new hardware.

Infinio Ultra R

A flexible, ultra-adaptive model powered by AutoSense OS™ 7.0, designed to automatically adjust to a wide range of environments. Ideal for patients seeking seamless performance in everyday listening.

Infinio Ultra Sphere

Engineered for complex and noisy situations, this model features Spheric Speech Clarity 2.0 and the breakthrough DEEPSONIC™ AI chip, the world’s first dedicated processor for real-time speech separation. This allows clearer understanding of multiple speakers from any direction, even in the most challenging soundscapes.

Unique Features:

  • AutoSense OS™ 7.0 adapts with 24% greater precision and is trained on 18× more sound scenarios, resulting in smarter environmental transitions and more natural listening.

  • Spheric Speech Clarity 2.0 (Ultra Sphere only) enhances speech up to threefold in noisy settings by isolating voices and reducing background interference.

Listening effort is reduced by up to 35%, supporting long-term comfort and decreasing auditory fatigue.

Phonak Virto Infinio

Phonak Virto Infinio is a custom in-the-ear (ITE) hearing aid released in 2025, built for those who want powerful performance in a discreet design. Each device is custom-molded to match the shape of your ear, giving you a snug, comfortable fit that lasts all day.

 

Packed with advanced sound technology, Virto Infinio delivers clear, natural hearing in a small, almost invisible shell, perfect for anyone who wants great sound without drawing attention.

 

Unique Features:

  • Custom-Made Fit: Each device is individually molded to the shape of your ear, ensuring a secure, comfortable fit that’s easy to wear all day.
  • AutoSense OS 6.0: Automatically adjusts settings based on your surroundings, providing seamless hearing whether you’re in quiet, noisy, or dynamic environments.
  • Durable & Water-Resistant: Rated IP68 for dust and moisture resistance, making it ideal for active lifestyles and daily wear.
  • Multiple Technology Levels: Available in four tiers (I90, I70, I50, I30) to meet a range of hearing needs and budgets.

Phonak Audéo Sphere

This smart hearing aid delivers clear, balanced sound and automatically adjusts to your surroundings. Whether you’re at home, in a busy café, or enjoying music, the Audéo Sphere adapts for an ideal hearing experience.

Key Features:

  • Rechargeable battery – All-day power with one charge
  • Smart sound processing – Separates speech from background noise
  • Bluetooth connectivity – Connects to phones for calls, music, and volume control

Phonak Audéo Infinio

This model boosts speech clarity in noisy settings while reducing listening fatigue. It also connects easily to smartphones and other devices.

Key Features:

  • CROS compatibility – Supports those with hearing loss in one ear
  • Roger Direct ready – Helps in loud environments and at a distance
  • Multiple Bluetooth connections – Pairs with up to 8 devices; connects to 2 at a time

Phonak CROS Infinio

Designed for people with hearing loss in one ear, this device sends sound from the non-hearing side to the better-hearing ear.

Key Features:

  • Hands-free calling and streaming – Works at longer ranges
  • Rechargeable battery – Convenient, long-lasting power
  • Universal Bluetooth – Compatible with most devices

Phonak Audéo Fit

Ideal for active individuals, the Audéo Fit monitors steps, activity levels, and heart rate through the myPhonak app.

Key Features:

  • Health tracking – Tracks movement and overall wellness
  • Tap control – Easily answer calls or change volume/settings
  • Bluetooth connectivity – Connects to phones, TVs, and more

Phonak Audéo Lumity

This model improves speech clarity using AI-based technology that adapts to your environment, making conversations easier in any setting.

Key Features:

  • Bluetooth connectivity – Connects to the myPhonak app and other devices
  • Customizable settings – Adjust preferences easily
  • Remote support – Get help from your audiologist without leaving home

Phonak Slim

Phonak Slim offers all the features of the Audéo Lumity in a sleek, stylish design. Its slim shape fits comfortably behind the ear-perfect for glasses wearers.

Key Features:

  • Ergonomic design – Comfortable and modern look
  • Bluetooth and app control – Easy adjustments on the go
  • Rechargeable battery – Full-day power

Phonak CROS Lumity

For single-sided hearing loss, the CROS Lumity transmits sound from your non-hearing side to your better-hearing ear, improving speech clarity.

Key Features:

  • Wireless sound transmission – Clear hearing from all directions
  • Bluetooth connectivity – Links with the myPhonak app and devices
  • Remote assistance – Audiologist support from home

Phonak Naída Lumity

Built for severe to profound hearing loss, this powerful device scans the environment 700 times per second for optimal sound quality.

Key Features:

  • Bluetooth connectivity – Pairs with phones and smart devices
  • Custom settings – Adjust through the myPhonak app
  • Multiple power options – Rechargeable and traditional battery models

Phonak Sky Lumity

Designed for children, the Sky Lumity helps kids hear better in classrooms, at home, and while playing.

Key Features:

  • SmartSpeech™ Technology – Enhances speech over background noise
  • Kid-friendly app – myPhonak Junior lets children control their settings
  • Tamper-resistant design – Safe and secure for young users

Phonak Virto Paradise

This discreet, custom-molded in-the-ear hearing aid improves speech clarity and reduces background noise.

Key Features:

  • Custom fit – Designed specifically for your ear shape
  • Bluetooth connectivity – Works with phones and TVs
  • Battery options – Available in different sizes

Phonak Assistive Devices

Phonak also offers helpful accessories for specific listening needs:

  • Phonak Serenity Choice – Protects ears from loud sounds while keeping speech clear
  • Roger On – Portable microphone for noisy places and group conversations
  • Roger Select – Table mic ideal for meetings and social events
  • TV Connector – Sends TV audio directly to your hearing aids
  • PartnerMic – Clip-on mic for one-on-one conversations

Older Phonak Hearing Aids We Service

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, we continue to support and repair earlier Phonak models, including:

  • Phonak Audéo Paradise
  • Phonak Naída Paradise
  • Phonak CROS Paradise
  • Phonak Naída Link Marvel
  • Phonak Sky Marvel
  • Phonak Audéo Marvel
  • Phonak Bolero B
  • Phonak Naída B
  • Phonak Virto B
  • Phonak Audéo B

Need repairs or support for older Phonak hearing aids? Contact us today!

Frequently Asked Questions About Phonak Hearing Aids

What are the different levels of Phonak Lumity?

Phonak Lumity comes in four levels: L90, L70, L50, and L30. L90 offers the most advanced features, while L30 provides a basic level of hearing support for everyday use.

What is Phonak Life?

Phonak Audéo Life™ Lumity is the first waterproof rechargeable hearing aid. It’s great for people who lead active lifestyles and need protection from water and sweat.

How is Phonak Life different from Phonak Paradise?

Phonak Life is more water-resistant than Phonak Paradise. It includes the same powerful features but adds extra durability for exercise, swimming, or wet environments.

Find the Right Phonak Hearing Aid for You

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, we help you choose the best Phonak hearing aid for your needs. We are an authorized provider of Phonak hearing aids in Granger, IN.

Contact us today to learn more!

Make an Appointment

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, our dedicated team of audiologists brings decades of experience in helping people hear and working with the latest hearing technology.

We go beyond a simple hearing test—our comprehensive evaluations assess hearing, cognition, vision, balance, and dexterity, ensuring a holistic approach to your care.

Let us help you get back to doing what you love with confidence.

Book Appointment

bodyHtml:
Skip to content

Privacy Policy

This Privacy Policy describes how your personal information is collected, used, and shared when you visit or make a purchase from (the “Site”).

PERSONAL INFORMATION WE COLLECT

When you visit the Site, we automatically collect certain information about your device, including information about your web browser, IP address, time zone, and some of the cookies that are installed on your device. Additionally, as you browse the Site, we collect information about the individual web pages or products that you view, what websites or search terms referred you to the Site, and information about how you interact with the Site. We refer to this automatically collected information as “Device Information.”

We collect Device Information using the following technologies:

– “Cookies” are data files that are placed on your device or computer and often include an anonymous unique identifier. For more information about cookies and how to disable cookies, visit http://www.allaboutcookies.org.
– “Log files” track actions occurring on the Site, and collect data, including your IP address, browser type, Internet service provider, referring/exit pages, and date/time stamps.
– “Web beacons,” “tags,” and “pixels” are electronic files used to record information about how you browse the Site.

Additionally, when you make a purchase or attempt to make a purchase through the Site, we collect certain information from you, including your name, billing address, shipping address, payment information (including credit card numbers), email address, and phone number. We refer to this information as “Order Information.”

When we talk about “Personal Information” in this Privacy Policy, we are talking both about Device Information and Order Information.

HOW DO WE USE YOUR PERSONAL INFORMATION?

We use the Device Information that we collect to help us screen for potential risk and fraud (in particular, your IP address), and more generally to improve and optimize our Site (for example, by generating analytics about how our customers browse and interact with the Site, and to assess the success of our marketing and advertising campaigns).

SHARING YOUR PERSONAL INFORMATION

We share your Personal Information with third parties to help us use your Personal Information, as described above.

We also use Google Analytics to help us understand how our customers use the Site–you can read more about how Google uses your Personal Information here: https://www.google.com/intl/en/policies/privacy/.

You can also opt out of Google Analytics here: https://tools.google.com/dlpage/gaoptout.

Finally, we may also share your Personal Information to comply with applicable laws and regulations, to respond to a subpoena, search warrant, or other lawful request for information we receive, or to otherwise protect our rights.

BEHAVIOURAL ADVERTISING

As described above, we use your Personal Information to provide you with targeted advertisements or marketing communications we believe may be of interest to you. For more information about how targeted advertising works, you can visit the Network Advertising Initiative’s (“NAI”) educational page at http://www.networkadvertising.org/understanding-online-advertising/how-does-it-work.

You can opt out of targeted advertising by:

FACEBOOK – https://www.facebook.com/settings/?tab=ads
GOOGLE – https://www.google.com/settings/ads/anonymous
BING – https://advertise.bingads.microsoft.com/en-us/resources/policies/personalized-ads

Additionally, you can opt out of some of these services by visiting the Digital Advertising Alliance’s opt-out portal at: http://optout.aboutads.info/.

DO NOT TRACK

Please note that we do not alter our Site’s data collection and use practices when we see a Do Not Track signal from your browser.

DATA RETENTION

When you place an order through the Site, we will maintain your Order Information for our records unless and until you ask us to delete this information.

CHANGES

We may update this privacy policy from time to time in order to reflect, for example, changes to our practices or for other operational, legal, or regulatory reasons.

CONTACT US

For more information about our privacy practices, if you have questions, or if you would like to make a complaint, please contact us by e-mail at forms@onephg.com or by mail using the details provided below:

209 Florence Ave. Granger, IN 46530
(574) 999-2853

Home › Hearing Aids › Rechargeable Hearing Aids: Easy, Eco-Friendly Hearing Care

Rechargeable Hearing Aids: Easy, Eco-Friendly Hearing Care

  • David A. Campbell, M.D
  • April 15, 2025

Tired of fumbling with tiny batteries or running out of power at the worst possible time? Rechargeable hearing aids might be the solution you’ve been waiting for. These modern devices offer a convenient, easy-to-use alternative to traditional battery-powered hearing aids. 

With just one overnight charge, you can enjoy a full day of clear, comfortable hearing. 

Rechargeable hearing aids are gaining popularity for a good reason—they eliminate the hassle of battery changes, make hearing care easier for those with vision or dexterity challenges, and provide reliable power throughout the day. If you’re looking for a hearing solution that fits into your lifestyle, this could be the perfect fit.

Campbell Hearing Solutions provides a wide selection of rechargeable hearing aids in Granger, IN. 

How Rechargeable Hearing Aids Work

Rechargeable hearing aids are powered by built-in lithium-ion batteries—the same reliable technology found in smartphones and tablets. Instead of replacing batteries every few days, you place your hearing aids in a charging station at night. By morning, they’re fully charged and ready to go. It’s a simple and worry-free routine.

Some models offer quick-charge features, giving users several hours of use in just 15 to 30 minutes—perfect if you forget to charge them overnight. These hearing aids also have smart power-saving modes to help conserve battery life throughout the day.

Benefits of Rechargeable Hearing Aids

  • Convenience: Just charge overnight and enjoy all-day power—no battery replacements needed.
  • Eco-Friendly: Say goodbye to throwing out batteries and reduce your environmental impact.
  • Cost-Effective: Save money over time by eliminating the need to buy disposable batteries.
  • Long Battery Life: Many models offer 16 to 30 hours of power on a full charge.
  • Easy to Use: Great for people with limited vision or hand mobility—no small parts to handle.

Who Can Benefit from Rechargeable Hearing Aids?

Rechargeable hearing aids are ideal for:

  • Busy adults and seniors who want a simpler routine
  • People with mild to profound hearing loss
  • Individuals with arthritis, tremors, or vision difficulties
  • Anyone tired of buying and replacing batteries

Whether you’re new to hearing aids or looking to upgrade, Campbell Hearing Solutions can help you find the right rechargeable option for your needs.

FAQs About Rechargeable Hearing Aids

How long does the battery last?

Most models offer a full day of use on a single charge.

How often do I need to charge my hearing aids?

It’s best to charge them every night as part of your bedtime routine.

What if I forget to charge them?

Some models offer quick charging, giving you hours of power in just 15-30 minutes.

Are rechargeable hearing aids water-resistant?

Yes! Most are water- and sweat-resistant, but not waterproof.

What happens when the battery wears out?

Rechargeable batteries typically last 3–5 years and can be replaced by your audiologist or the manufacturer.

Rechargeable Hearing Aids in Granger, IN

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, we specialize in the latest rechargeable hearing technology. Whether you’re exploring hearing aids for the first time or ready to switch from traditional batteries, our experienced audiologists in Granger, IN are here to help. We offer:

  • Personalized hearing evaluations and fittings
  • Ongoing maintenance and support
  • Cleanings and battery performance checks

We’ll make sure your hearing aids fit your lifestyle—and stay powered when you need them most. Let us help you hear better—without the hassle of batteries.

Contact us today to schedule your hearing test or consultation with expert audiologists in Granger, IN.

Make an Appointment

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, our dedicated team of audiologists brings decades of experience in helping people hear and working with the latest hearing technology.

We go beyond a simple hearing test—our comprehensive evaluations assess hearing, cognition, vision, balance, and dexterity, ensuring a holistic approach to your care.

Let us help you get back to doing what you love with confidence.
