bodyHtml:
Skip to content

Home › ReSound Hearing Aids

ReSound Hearing Aids

Campbell Hearing Solutions is an authorized provider of ReSound hearing aids in Granger, IN.

ReSound designs smart, innovative hearing aids to help you hear better every day. Whether you’re talking with loved ones, working, or spending time with friends, ReSound hearing aids make listening easier and more comfortable.

With cutting-edge technology, ReSound continues to improve the way people hear-providing clear, natural sound in all kinds of environments.

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, we proudly offer ReSound products to help you hear more, do more, and enjoy life to the fullest.

ReSound Hearing Aid Models Available In Granger, IN

ReSound ReSound Enzo IA™

The ReSound Enzo IA is designed for severe to profound hearing loss. It is the world’s smallest rechargeable Super Power hearing aid and the first of its kind to support Bluetooth LE Audio and Auracast broadcast streaming.

With a stylish, durable design, the ReSound Enzo IA is built for the most challenging listening situations. It offers exceptional clarity, feedback control, and all-day battery life. 

Unique Perks and Features:

  • Clear Focus: Automatically reduces background noise while highlighting speech.
  • 360 All-Around listening: Naturally shifts attention between voices and surrounding sounds.
  • Comfortable and weatherproof: Lightweight, all-day wear with protective nanocoating.
  • Flexible connectivity: Supports Bluetooth® LE Audio, Auracast™, and direct streaming from multiple devices.

ReSound Vivia™

ReSound Vivia™ is a smart hearing aid built to deliver natural, clear sound wherever you are. It uses brain-friendly technology to help you focus on what matters-whether you’re in a quiet room or a noisy setting.

Unique Features:

  • All-Day Battery Life – Keeps up with calls, music, and your day
  • Weather-Resistant Design – Protection from water and dust
  • Bluetooth & Auracast™ – Connects easily to phones, TVs, and public broadcasts
  • Auracast Assistant – Helps you hear announcements in places like theaters and airports

ReSound Savi™

ReSound Savi™ offers comfort, ease of use, and reliable performance. With a sleek design and smart connectivity, it helps you stay connected with clear, natural sound.

Unique Features:

  • Long-Lasting Battery – All-day power, even with streaming
  • Water and Dust Resistant – Built to last
  • Bluetooth & Auracast™ – Stream from your favorite devices
  • Auracast Assistant – Hear public audio announcements clearly
  • Hands-Free Calling – Answer calls with a simple tap

ReSound Nexia™

ReSound Nexia delivers clear, lifelike sound through its Organic Hearing™ system. It lets you stream calls, music, and video from iPhones, iPads, and select Android devices. You can even use it as a headset for Mac computers.

A CROS system is also available for people with hearing loss in one ear-it sends sound from the weaker ear to the stronger one.

Unique Features:

  • Advanced Bluetooth Streaming – Enhanced audio quality and battery efficiency
  • Auracast Broadcast Audio – Hear public broadcasts directly in your hearing aids
  • App & Remote Help – Adjust settings and manage tinnitus through the ReSound Smart 3D app

ReSound Omnia RIE & Mini RIE

ReSound Omnia provides natural sound and all-day comfort. Choose between Receiver-in-Ear (RIE) or Mini RIE styles-both great for mild to severe hearing loss. Rechargeable and battery-powered versions are available.

Unique Features:

  • Bluetooth Connectivity – Seamlessly connects to smartphones and tablets
  • Fitting Assistance – The Check My Fit tool helps ensure proper placement
  • App & Remote Help – Fine-tune your hearing aids from your phone
  • Tinnitus Relief – The ReSound Relief app offers sound therapy and relaxation exercises

ReSound Omnia ITE

ReSound Omnia also comes in In-The-Ear (ITE) styles for a custom fit. These models reduce wind noise and stay secure in your ear.

Available in:

  • In-the-Canal (ITC)
  • Completely-in-the-Canal (CIC)
  • Invisible-in-the-Canal (IIC)

 

Suitable for mild to profound hearing loss with rechargeable or battery-powered options.

Unique Features:

  • Bluetooth Connectivity – Pairs with iPhones, iPads, and Android devices
  • Fitting Assistance – Check My Fit ensures the best placement
  • App & Remote Help – Adjust sound settings and manage tinnitus from your phone
  • Tinnitus Relief – The ReSound Relief app provides relaxation and sound therapy

ReSound Omnia BTE & Power BTE

The Behind-the-Ear (BTE) models in the Omnia series are ideal for those with severe to profound hearing loss. These hearing aids improve speech understanding by up to 150% in noisy environments.

Unique Features:

  • Bluetooth Connectivity – Works with iPhones, iPads, and Android devices for hands-free calls
  • Fitting Assistance – Check My Fit ensures they are placed correctly
  • App & Remote Help – Adjust settings and fine-tune your hearing aids from your phone
  • Tinnitus Relief – The ReSound Relief app provides relaxation and sound therapy

ReSound Assistive Devices

ReSound also offers helpful accessories to make hearing easier in everyday situations:

  • TV Streamer 2 – Sends TV sound directly to your hearing aids
  • Phone Clip – Enables hands-free phone conversations
  • Multi Mic – Makes it easier to hear in noisy areas or meetings
  • Remote Control – Easily change volume or programs

What levels of technology are available with ReSound hearing aids?

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, we also continue to support and repair earlier ReSound models, including:

  • ReSound Key
  • ReSound ONE
  • ReSound LiNX Quattro
  • ReSound LiNX 3D
  • ReSound LiNX 2
  • ReSound Enzo Q
  • ReSound Enzo 2
  • ReSound Picasso

If you have an older device, we’re happy to help with repairs or maintenance. Please contact us to learn more.

What levels of technology are available with ReSound hearing aids?

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, we also continue to support and repair earlier ReSound models, including:

  • ReSound Key
  • ReSound ONE
  • ReSound LiNX Quattro
  • ReSound LiNX 3D
  • ReSound LiNX 2
  • ReSound Enzo Q
  • ReSound Enzo 2
  • ReSound Picasso

 

If you have an older device, we’re happy to help with repairs or maintenance. Please contact us to learn more.

Frequently Asked Questions About ReSound Hearing Aids

What levels of technology are available with ReSound hearing aids?

ReSound Omnia hearing aids come in three levels:

  • 9 (Premium) – Full-featured technology
  • 7 (Advanced) – Great balance of features and value
  • 5 (Basic) – Essential features for everyday hearing

How do I adjust my ReSound hearing aids?

You can use the ReSound Smart 3D app on your iPhone or Android device to adjust volume, settings, and even manage tinnitus.

How long do ReSound hearing aids last?

Most hearing aids last about five years, depending on care and usage. Many users upgrade sooner to take advantage of newer features and technology. Your audiologist can help you decide the right time to upgrade.

Find Your Perfect ReSound Hearing Aid in Granger, IN

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, we’ll help you find the best ReSound hearing aid for your hearing needs and lifestyle. We are an authorized ReSound provider serving the Granger, IN community.

Contact us today to schedule your consultation!

Make an Appointment

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, our dedicated team of audiologists brings decades of experience in helping people hear and working with the latest hearing technology.

We go beyond a simple hearing test—our comprehensive evaluations assess hearing, cognition, vision, balance, and dexterity, ensuring a holistic approach to your care.

Let us help you get back to doing what you love with confidence.

Sensorineural Hearing Loss Treatment in Granger, IN

  • David A. Campbell, M.D
  • May 17, 2025

Campbell Hearing Solutions provides sensorineural hearing loss treatment in Granger, IN.

Sensorineural Hearing Loss (SNHL) happens when the inner ear or the nerve that sends sound signals to the brain is damaged. This type of hearing loss makes it hard to hear soft sounds and can make louder sounds unclear or distorted. SNHL is usually permanent because the tiny hair cells in the inner ear do not grow back once they are damaged.

Why Seeking Treatment Is Important

If left untreated, SNHL can make it harder to understand conversations, enjoy daily activities, and stay connected with family and friends. It can also lead to social isolation, frustration, and even memory problems over time. The good news is that with the right treatment, you can improve your hearing and quality of life.

Symptoms of Sensorineural Hearing Loss

SNHL can develop gradually or suddenly. Some common signs include:

  • Difficulty hearing soft or whispered sounds
  • Trouble understanding speech – people may sound like they are mumbling
  • Frequently asking others to repeat themselves
  • Struggling to hear in noisy places like restaurants or group settings
  • Ringing in the ears (tinnitus)
  • Hearing better in one ear than the other
  • Feeling like sounds are muffled or far away
  • Difficulty hearing high-pitched sounds like children’s voices or birds

If you experience any of these symptoms, it’s important to schedule a hearing test with an audiologist. Early diagnosis can help you get the best treatment for your hearing needs.

Causes of Sensorineural Hearing Loss

SNHL can be caused by different factors, including:

  • Aging (Presbycusis) – Over time, the tiny hair cells in the inner ear wear down and stop working properly
  • Loud Noise Exposure – Repeated exposure to loud sounds like concerts, power tools, or gunfire can damage the inner ear
  • Genetics – Some people are born with SNHL or have a family history of hearing loss
  • Certain Medications – Some drugs, known as ototoxic medications, can damage the inner ear and cause hearing loss
  • Head Injuries – A strong blow to the head can harm the inner ear or the hearing nerve
  • Illnesses and Infections – Viruses, fevers, and autoimmune diseases can sometimes cause permanent hearing loss

Knowing the cause of your SNHL can help your audiologist recommend the most effective treatment for your specific needs.

Sensorineural Hearing Loss Diagnosis and Treatment

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, diagnosing SNHL starts with a hearing test performed by an audiologist. This test measures how well you hear different sounds and speech. If hearing loss is found, our Granger, IN audiologists will discuss the best treatment options to help you hear better.

Treatment Options for SNHL

  • Hearing Aids – These small devices make sounds louder and clearer. Many modern hearing aids include features like noise reduction, Bluetooth connectivity, and rechargeable batteries
  • Cochlear Implants – For those with severe hearing loss who don’t benefit from hearing aids, a cochlear implant may be recommended. This device sends sound signals directly to the hearing nerve
  • Bone-Anchored Hearing Aids (BAHA) – This device attaches to the bone behind the ear and helps people with single-sided deafness or mixed hearing loss

Your audiologist will help choose the best treatment based on your hearing loss and lifestyle needs.

What Is the Best Treatment for Sensorineural Hearing Loss?

The best treatment depends on how much hearing loss you have:

  • Mild to Moderate SNHL – Hearing aids are often the best choice
  • Severe SNHL – If hearing aids aren’t enough, cochlear implants may help
  • Single-Sided Deafness or Mixed Hearing Loss – A bone-anchored hearing aid (BAHA) may be the right solution

Our Granger, IN audiologists will work with you to find the hearing solution that fits you best.

Can You Fix Sensorineural Hearing Loss?

Right now, there is no cure for SNHL because the tiny hair cells in the inner ear do not grow back. But hearing aids and cochlear implants can help you hear better and improve your daily life. Getting treatment early gives your brain more time to adjust to hearing devices.

How Do You Prevent Further Sensorineural Hearing Loss?

While you can’t always prevent SNHL, you can take steps to protect your hearing:

  • Avoid loud noises – Wear hearing protection at concerts, while using power tools, or around gunfire
  • Turn down the volume – Keep music and TV at a safe level
  • Take listening breaks – Give your ears rest if you use headphones often
  • Check your medications – Ask your doctor if any of your medications could affect your hearing
  • Get regular hearing tests – Early detection helps slow hearing loss and improve treatment success

Protecting your hearing now can help you enjoy better hearing in the future.

Sensorineural Hearing Loss Treatment in Granger, IN

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, we know how important your hearing is to your everyday life. Our experienced audiologists offer: 

  • Comprehensive hearing tests to diagnose SNHL
  • Customized treatment plans made just for you
  • Advanced hearing aids, cochlear implants, and other solutions
  • Caring support focused on your hearing health

If you or someone you love is struggling with hearing loss, don’t wait. Early treatment can make a big difference in your hearing and quality of life.

Call us today to schedule an appointment!

Make an Appointment

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, our dedicated team of audiologists brings decades of experience in helping people hear and working with the latest hearing technology.

We go beyond a simple hearing test—our comprehensive evaluations assess hearing, cognition, vision, balance, and dexterity, ensuring a holistic approach to your care.

Let us help you get back to doing what you love with confidence.

Home › Should You Trust Nano Hearing Aids? An Audiologist’s Take

Should You Trust Nano Hearing Aids? An Audiologist’s Take

Nano hearing aids are small devices sold online that claim to help people hear better. You don’t need to visit a doctor or take a hearing test. Just go online, pick a model, and it’s shipped to your door.

This might sound fast and easy-but it can lead to problems. Nano hearing aids are not custom-made. They are “one-size-fits-all,” which means they may not work well for everyone. Some people say they help a little. Others say they’re too loud, don’t fit right, or stop working.

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, we believe your hearing care should be personal. Our audiologists in Granger, IN test your hearing and help you find the right solution, made just for you. Your ears-and your hearing-deserve expert care.

Pros and Cons of Buying Hearing Aids Online

Pros
  • Easy to order from home
  • Often less expensive
  • Arrives quickly

Cons
  • No hearing test or fitting
  • May not match your hearing needs
  • Can be uncomfortable
  • Limited support or customer service
  • Hard to return or adjust

Some people find Nano hearing aids work okay in quiet places. Others say the sound is poor, and the devices are hard to use or return. Reviews are mixed. Without an audiologist’s help, it’s hard to know if they’re right for you.

A Look at Nano Hearing Aids’ History

Nano Hearing Aids is a company that started a few years ago in the U.S. They advertise low-cost hearing aids that are small and easy to buy online. Many people saw their ads on social media or TV.

While the company is real, it has gotten a lot of complaints. Customers have said the sound is too loud or too soft, the devices are uncomfortable, or they couldn’t get help when something went wrong. The Better Business Bureau (BBB) has received many complaints about returns and service.

Nano doesn’t require a hearing test or doctor visit, which may sound simple-but hearing loss is personal. It’s not something to guess about. You need a professional to make sure you get the right help.

Are Nano Hearing Aids Good for Severe Hearing Loss?

If you have severe hearing loss, Nano hearing aids probably won’t help much.

These devices make all sounds louder, but they don’t know which sounds you need to hear-like someone talking to you-and which sounds you don’t-like background noise. This can make it hard to focus or understand speech, especially in noisy places.

Also, people with severe hearing loss often need:

  • Powerful hearing aids
  • Noise reduction
  • Speech focus technology
  • Bluetooth or rechargeable features
  • Help with ringing in the ears (tinnitus)

Nano hearing aids do not offer most of these features. They are not custom-fit to your ears, and they can’t be adjusted by an audiologist.

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, we test your hearing, explain the results, and fit hearing aids that match your hearing loss. We also teach you how to use them and provide ongoing support. You get better results-and better care-than with any online option.

Nano Hearing Aid Price Range

Nano hearing aids cost about $297 to $597 per pair. That price sounds low, but you don’t get the same features, comfort, or professional care that comes with hearing aids from a clinic.

Are Nano Hearing Aids a Scam?

Nano hearing aids are real products, but many people are unhappy with how they work. There are hundreds of complaints about sound quality, poor fit, weak batteries, and bad customer service.

Are they a scam? Not exactly. But they’re not the best choice either-especially if your hearing loss is moderate to severe.

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, we offer something better:

  • Full hearing tests by licensed audiologists
  • Hearing aids that fit your ears and match your needs
  • Follow-up care and adjustments
  • Help with cleaning, repairs, and comfort
  • Friendly, local service you can count on

We carry many kinds of hearing aids-from simple and affordable to high-tech models with features like Bluetooth and rechargeability. And we’ll be with you every step of the way.

Instead of guessing online, come see us first. We’ll help you find hearing solutions that truly work for you.

What Our Audiologists Say About Nano Hearing Aids

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, we believe your hearing deserves expert care. Nano hearing aids might seem like a good deal, but they skip the most important step-getting tested by an audiologist.

Hearing loss is not the same for everyone. What works for one person may not work for another. A hearing aid that’s too loud, too soft, or uncomfortable can make things worse.

We recommend starting with a hearing test. Our Granger, IN audiologists will explain everything and help you choose the best hearing aid for your life. You’ll get a better fit, better sound, and better support.

Call us today to schedule your hearing test!

Make an Appointment

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, our dedicated team of audiologists brings decades of experience in helping people hear and working with the latest hearing technology.

We go beyond a simple hearing test—our comprehensive evaluations assess hearing, cognition, vision, balance, and dexterity, ensuring a holistic approach to your care.

Let us help you get back to doing what you love with confidence.

bodyHtml:
Skip to content

Home › Signia Hearing Aids

Signia Hearing Aids

Campbell Hearing Solutions is an authorized provider of Signia hearing aids in Granger, IN.

Campbell Hearing Solutions is proud to be an authorized provider of Signia hearing aids in Granger, IN. Signia believes in helping people “Hear Well and Be Well.” Their advanced hearing aid technology is designed not only to improve hearing but also to support your overall quality of life.

One of their latest innovations, Augmented Xperience (AX), helps you hear speech more clearly-even when background noise is loud.

With a wide range of styles and technology levels, Signia offers options to fit nearly every hearing need and lifestyle.

Current Signia Hearing Aid Models in Granger, IN

Signia Pure Charge&Go BCT IX

Signia Pure Charge&Go BCT IX is a stylish receiver-in-canal (RIC) hearing aid that makes it easy to stay connected. With built-in Bluetooth Classic, you can enjoy hands-free calls and audio streaming from your favorite devices, like smartphones, tablets, TVs, and computers.

Designed for active lifestyles, this hearing aid delivers powerful performance, all-day battery life, and clear conversations no matter where you are. It’s the perfect choice for anyone who wants reliable hearing support with modern, wireless convenience.

Unique features:

  • Universal Bluetooth Classic Connectivity: Easy pairing and reliable audio streaming.
  • Long battery usage: Up to 36 hours of battery life on a single charge
  • Speech understanding in noisy environments: Powered by Integrated Xperience (IX) platform with RealTime Conversation Enhancement technology.
  • Discreet design: Sleek, discreet design that ranks among the smallest Bluetooth-compatible RIC devices available.
  • Built-in telecoil: For optimized performance in public venues.

Styletto IX

The Styletto IX combines elegant design with advanced hearing support. It’s ideal for individuals looking for a stylish and modern hearing aid with smart features.

Key Features:

  • Improved speech clarity in noisy environments
  • AI-powered sound adjustments based on your surroundings

 

Unique Benefits:

  • Rechargeable: Up to 3 days of power using the portable charging case
  • Bluetooth Connectivity: Stream calls, music, and media from your smartphone
  • RealTime Conversation Enhancement: Automatically adapts to help you follow conversations more easily

Pure Charge&Go IX

This popular model blends excellent sound quality with everyday convenience. It’s one of Signia’s top rechargeable hearing aids.

Key Features:

  • Crystal-clear speech understanding in noisy areas
  • Personalized sound adjustments powered by AI

 

Unique Benefits:

  • Bluetooth Streaming: Connects to phones, TVs, and more
  • Rechargeable: Long-lasting power throughout the day
  • All-Day Comfort: Lightweight and discreet design

Silk Charge&Go IX

Designed for comfort and instant wear, this model fits snugly without the need for a custom mold.

Unique Benefits:

  • Instant Fit: Soft silicone Click Sleeves for a quick, secure fit
  • Clear Audio Quality: Focuses on voices, reduces background noise
  • Bluetooth Streaming: Easily stream from smartphones and devices

Insio IX IIC/CIC

These custom-made hearing aids fit deep in the ear and are nearly invisible when worn.

Unique Benefits:

  • Discreet Look: Tiny design for a low-profile appearance
  • Custom Fit: Tailored to your ear shape for all-day comfort
  • App Control: Adjust settings with the Signia app

Signia Pure Charge&Go T AX

This RITE (receiver-in-the-ear) model uses Augmented Focus™ to separate speech from noise, making it easier to understand conversations in any setting. It’s ideal for mild to severe hearing loss.

Unique Benefits:

  • Bluetooth Streaming: Direct connection to iPhone and Android
  • Rechargeable: Up to 36 hours of use per charge
  • App + Remote Support: Control your hearing aid and access Signia Assistant and TeleCare anytime

Styletto AX

This sleek and rechargeable option offers similar performance to the Pure AX series, with a more stylish design.

Unique Benefits:

  • Bluetooth Enabled: Stream audio from your devices
  • Wireless Charging: No cords needed-just place in the charging case
  • Up to 20 Hours of Use per Charge

Insio Charge&Go AX

Signia’s custom-fit, rechargeable hearing aids provide clear sound and easy charging-without wires. These are a great choice for people with mild to severe hearing loss.

Unique Benefits:

  • Bluetooth Streaming: Hear phone calls, music, and videos directly
  • Wireless Charging: Just place in the charger-no ports or plugs
  • App Access: Manage settings and get help through your smartphone

Signia Motion X

A powerful behind-the-ear (BTE) hearing aid built for long battery life and comfort. It’s ideal for people with moderate to profound hearing loss.

Unique Benefits:

  • Up to 61 Hours of Power: Extended listening without recharging
  • Bluetooth Ready: Pairs easily with smartphones and devices
  • Tinnitus Relief: Built-in sound therapy for ringing in the ears

Signia CROS AX

Designed for those with hearing loss in one ear, CROS AX sends sound from the weaker ear to the stronger one.

Available Styles:

  • CROS Styletto AX: Slim and stylish; 20 hours per charge
  • CROS Pure Charge&Go AX: Rechargeable; up to 29 hours of use
  • CROS Pure 312: Disposable battery model; up to 4 days battery life
  • CROS Silk X: Small, comfortable, and ready-to-wear

 

Unique Benefits:

  • Bluetooth Compatible: Works with Apple and Android
  • Easy App Control: Adjust settings and track wellness with the Signia app

Signia Assistive Listening Devices & Accessories
  • StreamLine TV: Sends TV audio directly to your hearing aids
  • StreamLine Mic: Acts as a microphone or hands-free device for calls
  • StreamLine Remote: Simple remote control for volume and program changes

Older Signia Models We Support

Campbell Hearing Solutions can also service or assist with older models such as:

  • Signia Pure Charge&Go X
  • Signia Pure 312 X
  • Signia CROS X
  • Signia Intuis 3
  • Signia Insio Nx
  • Signia Pure Nx
  • Signia Silk Nx
  • Signia Silk Primax
  • Signia Cellion Primax
  • Signia Pure Primax

Please contact us for more information.

Frequently Asked Questions About Signia Hearing Aids

What is the most advanced Signia hearing aid?

Signia’s latest technology is found in their AX platform, including Styletto AX, Pure AX, and Insio Charge&Go AX. These models use Augmented Focus™ to separate speech from noise, making conversations clearer in every setting.

What are the technology levels of Signia Pure Charge&Go AX?

Signia offers five technology levels: 1AX, 2AX, 3AX, 5AX, and 7AX. The higher the level, the more advanced features and sound customization you get.

Can I adjust my Signia hearing aids myself?

Yes! The Signia app allows you to change volume, switch programs, and even fine-tune sound quality (like bass and treble) directly from your smartphone.

Signia Hearing Aids in Granger, IN

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, we’re here to help you find the right hearing solution for your lifestyle. Whether you’re new to hearing aids or ready to upgrade to the latest Signia technology, our Granger, IN audiologists are ready to help.

Contact us today to schedule a consultation!

Make an Appointment

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, our dedicated team of audiologists brings decades of experience in helping people hear and working with the latest hearing technology.

We go beyond a simple hearing test—our comprehensive evaluations assess hearing, cognition, vision, balance, and dexterity, ensuring a holistic approach to your care.

Let us help you get back to doing what you love with confidence.

Book Appointment

bodyHtml:
Skip to content

Home › Starkey Hearing Aids

Starkey Hearing Aids

Campbell Hearing Solutions is an authorized provider of Starkey hearing aids in Granger, IN.

Starkey is a trusted leader in hearing technology, known for creating advanced hearing aids that help people hear better in all types of situations. Starkey hearing aids are small, custom-fit, and comfortable, making them a reliable choice for a variety of listening environments.

Starkey also uses artificial intelligence (AI) to improve sound quality and make listening easier. Their wide range of hearing aids supports different hearing needs and lifestyles.

Current Starkey Hearing Aid Models

Starkey Omega AI

Starkey Omega AI is built with industry-leading technology that gives users powerful sound clarity and day-long comfort. Its advanced AI system constantly analyzes your surroundings to help you hear speech clearly, stay aware of important sounds, and move through different environments with ease.

 

If you struggle with hearing in noisy places, Omega AI’s Edge Mode+ provides on-demand help by sharpening speech instantly, even in challenging spaces like restaurants, shopping centers, or family gatherings.

 

These hearing aids are also designed for durability. With a 10x more durable waterproof coating, Omega AI can handle rain, sweat, and everyday wear without worry. Rechargeable options give all-day power, keeping you connected from morning to night.

 

Unique Features:

 

  • Edge Mode+: Boosts speech clarity during tough listening moments.
  • Waterproof & Everyday-Proof: Designed for active lifestyles and all-day comfort.
  • Auracast™ & LE Audio Ready: Allows streaming from TVs, smartphones, public announcement systems, and more.
  • Starkey App (Now Powered by Gen AI): Use TeleHear AI for instant, AI-based sound adjustments.

Starkey Signature Series

The Starkey Signature Series gives you amazing sound in hearing aids that are super tiny and almost invisible. They are custom-made to fit inside your ear, so no one can tell you’re wearing them. You’ll hear your friends, family, and the world around you clearly, without anyone knowing why. Whether you want rechargeable, adjustable, or the smallest size possible, there’s a signature style just for you.

 

Unique Features:

 

  • Discreet: Nearly invisible and fits deep in your ear.
  • Natural Sound: Clear, lifelike sound so you can enjoy every moment.
  • Rechargeable: Up to 38 hours of power—no tiny batteries to change.
  • Waterproof: Designed to handle water up to 1 meter deep.
  • Custom Fit: Made just for your ears for all-day comfort and security.

Starkey Edge AI

Starkey Edge AI hearing aids use advanced AI technology to improve sound clarity and reduce background noise. A powerful chip helps you focus on speech, even in noisy places like restaurants or parties.

These hearing aids automatically adjust to your surroundings, offering speech clarity up to 30% better than older models.

Unique Features:

  • Built to Last: Waterproof up to 1 meter and resistant to sweat
  • Strong Bluetooth Connection: Pairs with smartphones and supports Auracast™ for TV and public audio streaming
  • Health & Safety Tools: Includes balance tracking and fall detection
  • Easy App Control: Use the My Starkey app to adjust settings, control volume, stream calls, and locate lost hearing aids (compatible with smartphones and Apple Watch)

Starkey Genesis AI RIC

Starkey Genesis AI hearing aids use intelligent technology to help you hear soft sounds more clearly, understand speech better, and reduce listening effort. These receiver-in-canal (RIC) hearing aids work well for mild to severe hearing loss.

They are available with rechargeable or disposable batteries. The rechargeable version offers up to 51 hours of use on a full charge and provides up to 3 hours of power with just a 7-minute charge.

Unique Features:

  • Waterproof: Protective coating guards against sweat, rain, and debris
  • Bluetooth Connectivity: Use the My Starkey app for settings, streaming, and device location
  • Remote Support: Get help through TeleHear video appointments
  • Tinnitus Relief: Custom sound therapy to ease ringing in the ears

Starkey Genesis AI ITE

The Genesis AI ITE (in-the-ear) hearing aid is custom-made to fit your ear comfortably and securely. Its smart processor mimics the way the brain processes sound, offering natural clarity in any setting.

This model works for mild to severe hearing loss and is available in rechargeable or disposable battery options. It includes Starkey’s smallest wireless CIC (completely-in-canal) model for a nearly invisible fit.

Unique Features:

  • Waterproof: Protective layers shield against water, sweat, and dust
  • Bluetooth Connectivity: Pairs with smartphones for easy control
  • Remote Support: Adjustments available through video support
  • Tinnitus Relief: Personalized sound options to reduce ear ringing

Starkey CROS

Starkey CROS hearing aids are designed for individuals with hearing loss in one ear. They capture sound from the non-hearing side and send it to the better-hearing ear, helping improve conversation and sound awareness.

These hearing aids are compatible with Genesis AI models and select previous Starkey models.

Starkey Evolv AI BTE

Starkey Evolv AI behind-the-ear (BTE) hearing aids adjust automatically to your environment, making it easier to hear clearly in different situations. These hearing aids perform 55 million adjustments per hour to reduce background noise like wind or mechanical sounds.

Suitable for mild to severe hearing loss, they are available in rechargeable or disposable battery options.

Unique Features:

  • Bluetooth Connectivity: Use the Thrive Hearing Control app to adjust settings
  • Remote Support: Receive help through video consultations
  • Tinnitus Relief: Built-in sound programs to help with ear ringing

Starkey Evolv AI Power Plus BTE

The Evolv AI Power Plus BTE offers the same features as the standard Evolv AI BTE but with extra amplification for severe to profound hearing loss. This model uses size 13 disposable batteries for longer-lasting power.

Unique Features:

  • Bluetooth Connectivity: Compatible with the Thrive Hearing Control app
  • Remote Support: Adjustments made through virtual appointments
  • Tinnitus Relief: Custom sound options for tinnitus management

Assistive Listening Devices & Technology by Starkey
  • Mini Remote Microphone
  • Remote Mic +
  • Remote Control 2.0
  • Table Microphone
  • TV Streamer

Older Starkey Hearing Aids Supported By Campbell Hearing Solutions

If you have an older Starkey hearing aid, Campbell Hearing Solutions may still be able to service or repair it. Supported models include:

  • Starkey Picasso
  • Starkey Livio
  • Starkey Muse iQ
  • Starkey Halo iQ
  • Starkey Halo 2
  • Starkey Halo

Please contact us to learn more about service options for older models.

Frequently Asked Questions About Starkey Hearing Aids

What are the tech levels of Starkey Genesis AI?

Starkey Genesis AI hearing aids come in four technology levels:

  • 24 (Premium)
  • 20 (Advanced)
  • 16 (Standard)
  • 12 (Basic)A CROS model is also available for individuals with hearing loss in one ear.

What app does Starkey Genesis AI use?

The My Starkey app pairs with Genesis AI hearing aids. It allows users to adjust settings, track health data, and locate lost hearing aids.

How long do Starkey hearing aids last?

In-the-ear hearing aids typically last around five years, while behind-the-ear models often last five to six years. With proper care, they may last longer. Many people choose to upgrade earlier to benefit from the latest technology. Your audiologist can help determine when it’s time for new hearing aids.

Find the Right Starkey Hearing Aid for You

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, we help you choose the best Starkey hearing aid for your needs.

We are an authorized provider of Starkey hearing aids in Granger, IN.

Contact us today to learn more!

Make an Appointment

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, our dedicated team of audiologists brings decades of experience in helping people hear and working with the latest hearing technology.

We go beyond a simple hearing test—our comprehensive evaluations assess hearing, cognition, vision, balance, and dexterity, ensuring a holistic approach to your care.

Let us help you get back to doing what you love with confidence.

Home › Hearing Aids › Swimmer’s Ear: Causes, Symptoms, and Treatment

Swimmer’s Ear: Causes, Symptoms, and Treatment

  • David A. Campbell, M.D
  • April 9, 2025

Swimmer’s ear, also known as otitis externa, is an infection of the outer ear canal that often develops when moisture becomes trapped inside the ear. This creates a warm, damp environment where bacteria or fungi can thrive, causing irritation and inflammation. While swimming is a common cause, swimmer’s ear can happen to anyone who is exposed to excess moisture, ear trauma, or skin irritation in the ear canal.

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, our Granger, IN audiologists are experienced in identifying and managing conditions like swimmer’s ear. Whether you’re an avid swimmer or just experiencing persistent ear discomfort, we’re here to help you find relief and prevent complications.

What Causes Swimmer’s Ear?

Swimmer’s ear occurs when your ear’s built-in defense system is weakened. Under normal conditions, your ears can handle moisture thanks to earwax, the natural acidity of the ear canal, and its curved structure. However, several factors can disrupt this balance and open the door to infection.

Common causes include:

  • Water trapped in the ear after swimming or showering
  • Damage from cotton swabs, fingernails, or other objects
  • Allergic reactions to hair products, earrings, or skin care products
  • Irritation from hearing aids, earbuds, or foam earplugs

Your Ear’s Natural Defenses

Your ears are designed to protect themselves. Cerumen (ear wax) plays an essential role in:

  • Trapping dust, bacteria, and foreign particles
  • Preventing microbial growth
  • Repelling water thanks to its oily nature

In addition, the curved shape of the ear canal helps prevent deep water intrusion. But when these defenses are disrupted—by overcleaning, frequent water exposure, or skin irritation—it’s easier for infections like swimmer’s ear to take hold.

Why Do People Get Swimmer’s Ear?

Swimmer’s ear can happen to anyone, but certain habits or conditions increase your risk. You may be more prone to developing it if you:

  • Swim frequently, especially in warm or untreated water
  • Use cotton swabs or other objects to clean your ears
  • Have skin conditions like eczema or dermatitis
  • Wear hearing aids or earbuds that cause friction or trap moisture

Symptoms of Swimmer’s Ear

Swimmer’s ear symptoms can vary in severity:

Mild Symptoms:

  • Itching in the ear canal
  • Mild discomfort
  • Slight redness
  • Clear, odorless drainage

Moderate Symptoms:

  • Increased pain when touching the ear
  • More pronounced swelling and redness
  • Drainage that becomes cloudy or yellow

Severe Symptoms:

  • Intense pain that may radiate to the neck or face
  • Swollen lymph nodes
  • Fever
  • Complete blockage of the ear canal

How Is Swimmer’s Ear Diagnosed?

A professional evaluation is essential. At Campbell Hearing Solutions, we use an otoscope to examine the ear canal for redness, swelling, and fluid. Gentle manipulation of the outer ear may also reveal tenderness—a classic sign of swimmer’s ear. In persistent or complicated cases, fluid may be tested to identify the exact source of the infection.

How Is Swimmer’s Ear Treated?

Most cases of swimmer’s ear respond well to treatment. Your provider may:

  • Prescribe antibiotic ear drops to fight infection
  • Recommend steroid drops to reduce inflammation
  • Suggest over-the-counter pain relievers for comfort

If the ear canal is swollen shut, a wick may be inserted to help deliver medication deeper into the ear. It’s important to keep the ear dry while healing and avoid inserting anything into the ear canal.

Severe or unresponsive cases may require oral antibiotics or a referral to an ear specialist.

Can Swimmer’s Ear Be Prevented?

Absolutely! You can greatly reduce your chances of developing swimmer’s ear with a few simple precautions:

  • Dry your ears thoroughly after water activities
  • Tilt your head to help drain trapped water
  • Avoid inserting cotton swabs, earbuds, or other objects
  • Use waterproof earplugs or swim caps while swimming
  • Steer clear of harsh hair products or allergens near the ears
  • Give ears time to heal after infections or injuries before returning to the water

Who’s at Risk For Swimmer’s Ear?

You may be more susceptible to swimmer’s ear if you:

  • Swim often, especially in lakes, pools, or hot tubs
  • Clean your ears too aggressively
  • Have a history of ear infections (recurrent)
  • Wear hearing aids or earbuds regularly
  • Have narrow ear canals that trap moisture
  • Suffer from chronic skin conditions

Swimmer’s Ear Potential Complications

If left untreated, swimmer’s ear can lead to more serious problems, such as:

  • Temporary hearing loss due to swelling and fluid
  • Chronic otitis externa, or recurring infections
  • Cellulitis, a deeper skin infection
  • Spread of infection to nearby tissues and nerves in extreme cases
  • Malignant otitis externa, a rare but severe condition affecting the skull base (primarily in older adults or those with diabetes)

Prompt treatment is key to avoiding these complications.

Don’t Ignore the Itch and Pain, Seek Professional Help

Swimmer’s ear may start with just a mild itch or irritation, but without treatment, it can quickly worsen. If you’re experiencing ear pain, discharge, or hearing changes, it’s time to seek professional help.

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, our team is committed to helping you stay comfortable, healthy, and protected from preventable ear conditions.

Call today to schedule your appointment with expert audiologists in Granger, IN.

      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Will tinnitus go away on its own?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Tinnitus may improve over time, especially if caused by temporary factors like noise exposure or earwax. If symptoms persist for several weeks, it is best to consult an audiologist."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Can stress make tinnitus worse?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Yes. Increased stress and anxiety can heighten your awareness of tinnitus. Reducing stress can often help lessen its effects."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Is tinnitus always linked to hearing loss?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Not always. Many individuals with tinnitus do have hearing loss, but it is also possible to experience tinnitus with normal hearing. A hearing evaluation can help determine any underlying issues."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Do hearing aids help with tinnitus?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Yes. Hearing aids can enhance external sounds, making tinnitus less noticeable. Many models also include sound therapy features that help reduce the impact of ringing or buzzing."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Can earwax cause tinnitus?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Yes. A buildup of earwax can block sound and lead to tinnitus. Professional earwax removal may help relieve symptoms if wax is the cause."
      }

bodyHtml:
Skip to content

Home › Tinnitus Support

Tinnitus Support

Campbell Hearing Solutions provides expert tinnitus support in Granger, IN.

Tinnitus is the perception of sound-such as ringing, buzzing, hissing, or clicking-in the ears when there is no actual external noise. It can be mild and occasional or persistent and disruptive. For some individuals, tinnitus can interfere with concentration, sleep, or overall quality of life.

What Causes Tinnitus?

Tinnitus can result from a variety of causes, including:

  • Hearing loss – When the brain receives reduced sound input, it may create phantom sounds.
  • Exposure to loud noise – Loud music, machinery, or other loud environments can damage hearing.
  • Earwax buildup – Excess earwax can block the ear canal and lead to ringing sounds.
  • Medications – Some medications list tinnitus as a possible side effect.
  • Medical conditions – Issues such as high blood pressure, ear infections, or temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders may contribute to tinnitus.

Tinnitus Support Options

While there is no permanent cure for tinnitus, several treatment options can help reduce symptoms and improve comfort.

Sound Therapy

Using background noise can help make tinnitus less noticeable. Options may include:

  • White noise machines
  • Soft background music or a fan
  • Hearing aids with built-in sound masking features

Hearing Aids

For individuals with hearing loss, hearing aids can improve overall sound clarity, which may reduce the perception of tinnitus. Many hearing aids also offer sound masking features to help manage symptoms.

Stress Management and Counseling

Stress and anxiety can worsen tinnitus. Managing stress can help reduce its impact. Options may include:

  • Deep breathing or meditation
  • Gentle exercises such as yoga
  • Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT) to develop helpful coping strategies

Lifestyle Changes

Certain daily habits can influence tinnitus. Consider the following:

  • Limit caffeine and alcohol intake
  • Use hearing protection in noisy environments
  • Practice relaxation techniques regularly
  • Maintain a consistent sleep schedule

Frequently Asked Questions About Tinnitus

Will tinnitus go away on its own?

Tinnitus may improve over time, especially if caused by temporary factors like noise exposure or earwax. If symptoms persist for several weeks, it is best to consult an audiologist.

Can stress make tinnitus worse?

Yes. Increased stress and anxiety can heighten your awareness of tinnitus. Reducing stress can often help lessen its effects.

Is tinnitus always linked to hearing loss?

Not always. Many individuals with tinnitus do have hearing loss, but it is also possible to experience tinnitus with normal hearing. A hearing evaluation can help determine any underlying issues.

Do hearing aids help with tinnitus?

Yes. Hearing aids can enhance external sounds, making tinnitus less noticeable. Many models also include sound therapy features that help reduce the impact of ringing or buzzing.

Can earwax cause tinnitus?

Yes. A buildup of earwax can block sound and lead to tinnitus. A medical evaluation may help relieve symptoms if wax buildup is the cause.

Tinnitus Support in Granger, IN

Our audiologists at Campbell Hearing Solutions, are here to help with expert tinnitus support options to make the ringing in your ears more manageable.

Call us today to schedule an appointment tin Granger, IN!

Make an Appointment

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, our dedicated team of audiologists brings decades of experience in helping people hear and working with the latest hearing technology.

We go beyond a simple hearing test—our comprehensive evaluations assess hearing, cognition, vision, balance, and dexterity, ensuring a holistic approach to your care.

Let us help you get back to doing what you love with confidence.

Home › Hearing Aids › Understanding Pressure in the Ear: Causes, Treatments, and When to Seek Help

Understanding Pressure in the Ear: Causes, Treatments, and When to Seek Help

  • David A. Campbell, M.D
  • September 18, 2025

Have you ever felt like your ears were “plugged up” or ready to pop, even when there was no loud noise around? That sensation of pressure in the ear can feel like fullness, tightness, or a dull ache.

While it might seem harmless at first (and it actually is in most cases), pressure in the ears can point to many underlying causes.

An audiologist can diagnose and manage pressure in the ears.  

Campbell Hearing Solutions connects you to expert audiologists in Granger, IN, who can help you find the cause and get the relief you need.

Causes of Ear Pressure

Ear Wax Buildup

One of the most common and easily treatable causes of ear pressure is excessive ear wax. 

When wax builds up too much, it can block the ear canal and create a sensation of fullness or pressure.

While it’s tempting to use cotton swabs or at-home remedies, these can often make the problem worse by pushing wax deeper. Professional ear cleaning by an audiologist is the safest and most effective solution.

Campbell Hearing Solutions provides professional ear-related symptom guidance in Granger, IN.

Eustachian Tube Dysfunction

The Eustachian tubes are small passageways that connect the middle ear to the back of the throat. Their job is to equalize air pressure on both sides of the eardrum. When these tubes become blocked (often due to allergies, sinus infections, or colds) it can result in that familiar feeling of ear pressure.

This condition can also cause popping sounds, discomfort during altitude changes, or a clogged sensation. Audiologists can assess Eustachian tube function and recommend therapies or refer you to a specialist for further treatment.

Middle Ear Infections (Otitis Media)

Ear infections in the middle ear occur when fluid becomes trapped behind the eardrum. This condition is more common in children, but adults can experience it too.

It causes pressure, pain, and sometimes temporary hearing loss.

Barotrauma

If you’ve ever felt ear pressure while flying or scuba diving, you’ve experienced barotrauma, a pressure-related ear injury. This happens when rapid altitude or pressure changes affect the middle ear and the Eustachian tubes can’t equalize quickly enough.

While it usually resolves on its own, persistent symptoms following travel or diving should be evaluated by a hearing professional to rule out lasting damage.

Meniere’s Disease

Meniere’s disease is a chronic condition that affects the inner ear and often includes ear pressure as a primary symptom. It’s typically accompanied by dizziness, tinnitus, and hearing loss.

Because it affects balance and hearing, an audiologist plays a key role in diagnosing and managing Meniere’s disease with specialized testing and customized hearing care.

Why You Should See an Audiologist for Ear Pressure

Audiologists use advanced equipment to examine your ear canal, eardrum, and hearing system. This allows them to determine the exact cause of your ear pressure.

Audiologists provide care tailored to your condition.

If needed, they can refer you to ENT specialists for medical treatment or surgery.

Campbell Hearing Solutions connects you to an expert audiologist in Granger, IN.

Hearing Protection and Monitoring

Chronic ear pressure can sometimes lead to or signal hearing damage. Audiologists are equipped to monitor your hearing health and recommend protective strategies.

Comprehensive Audiology Care

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, we offer holistic care, from diagnosis to follow-up, ensuring that your ear pressure is managed properly and your hearing remains protected.

Treating Pressure in the Ear

  • Professional Ear Evaluation: Professional assessment to determine whether blockage is contributing to symptoms.
  • Medications for Infections: Audiologists coordinate with physicians to treat infections with antibiotics or anti-inflammatories.
  • Hearing Aids or Specialized Devices: For chronic conditions like Meniere’s or pressure due to Eustachian tube issues, hearing aids or pressure-regulating devices may be recommended.

When to Seek Immediate Help

Red Flag Symptoms include:

  • Sudden or severe hearing loss
  • Sharp or prolonged ear pain
  • Dizziness or balance problems
  • Ringing in the ears (tinnitus)

If you’re experiencing these symptoms along with ear pressure, seek help right away. 

Audiologists are your first line of care, ensuring fast, accurate diagnosis and proper treatment.

Preventing Pressure In Ears

  • Avoid Using Cotton Swabs: They can push wax deeper or damage the ear canal.
  • Protect Ears During Altitude Changes: Try yawning, swallowing, or chewing gum when flying or driving through elevations.
  • Manage Allergies or Sinus Issues: Controlling inflammation can help reduce Eustachian tube blockages and prevent ear pressure.

Audiologists in Granger, IN

Persistent or recurring ear pressure isn’t something to ignore. At Dr. Campbell Hearing Solutions, we specialize in identifying the root causes and providing effective, customized care for each patient.

Our trusted Granger, IN audiologists are here to help you feel your best and hear your best. 
Contact us today to schedule a consultation or hearing test.

Make an Appointment

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, our dedicated team of audiologists brings decades of experience in helping people hear and working with the latest hearing technology.

We go beyond a simple hearing test—our comprehensive evaluations assess hearing, cognition, vision, balance, and dexterity, ensuring a holistic approach to your care.

Let us help you get back to doing what you love with confidence.

Home › Unitron Hearing Aids

Unitron Hearing Aids

Campbell Hearing Solutions is an authorized provider of Unitron hearing aids in Granger, IN.

Unitron is a trusted Canadian company that has been making hearing aids for over 50 years. Their smart technology automatically adjusts to different environments.

Unitron hearing aids are designed to help people hear better in different situations. They provide a comfortable fit, clear sound, and easy-to-use features.

Current Unitron Hearing Aid Models

Below are the latest Unitron hearing aids available at Campbell Hearing Solutions:

Unitron Insera S-R

Powered by the advanced Smile platform, the Insera S-R is meticulously crafted to fit the unique contours of your ear, offering a discreet design without compromising on power. Whether you’re in a crowded restaurant or a quiet home, its AI-driven technology automatically adjusts to your environment, making hearing feel natural and effortless.

It comes with robust Bluetooth connectivity and all-day battery life to help you stay connected to the people and devices that matter most.

Unique Perks and Features:

  • EarMatch™ Customization: An evolved design process that uses deep-fit modeling and intentional microphone placement to optimize sound performance specifically for your unique ear anatomy.
  • Universal Bluetooth® Connectivity: Enjoy stable, hands-free calls and high-quality media streaming. The Insera S-R features up to 6x more wireless transmission power and double the connection range of previous models.
  • Rechargeable Custom Fit: Combines the “invisible” appeal of a custom-molded hearing aid with the modern convenience of lithium-ion reliability and a magnetic “snap-and-charge” docking station.
  • HyperFocus & Speech Enhancement: Advanced spatial features that zoom in on speech during loud conversations while reducing the listening effort required in quieter settings to prevent “listening fatigue.”
  • IP68-Rated Durability: Engineered for everyday life with proven wax protection and a rugged, water-resistant design that ensures dependable performance in all conditions.

Unitron Moxi S-R

The Unitron Moxi S-R is a small, stylish hearing aid that’s made to fit comfortably and work reliably every day. It’s powered by Unitron’s newest Smile platform, which helps you hear clearly in more places and enjoy life’s sounds with ease. 

Moxi S-R is also built to handle water and moisture, so you don’t have to worry about splashes or weather. With its easy-to-use charger and a portable ChargerGo, you can keep your hearing aids powered, whether you’re at home or on the move.

Unique Features:

  • Water-Resistant: Special coating helps protect from moisture.
  • Easy Charging: Simple charging at home or on-the-go with ChargerGo.
  • All-Day Power: Keeps up with your busy life without running out of charge.

Unitron Ativo

Unitron Ativo hearing aids give you clear sound and wireless connection at a price that’s easy to love. You can take hands-free phone calls, stream music or videos, and enjoy great hearing – all in a design that’s both comfortable and stylish. Ativo helps you stay connected to your favorite people, sounds, and moments every day.

Unique Features:

  • Bluetooth Connectivity: Take calls and stream sound straight from your phone.
  • Clear Sound: Enjoy Unitron’s reliable sound performance in any setting.
  • Hands-Free Calling: Talk on the phone without holding it up.
  • Stylish and Comfortable: Designed to look good and feel good all day.
  • Affordable Quality: Great technology at an easy-to-access price.

Unitron Moxi V-RS

The Unitron Moxi V-RS is a lightweight hearing aid that sits behind the ear. Even though it is small, it makes sound clear and natural in both quiet and noisy places.

  • Bluetooth Connection: Connects to your phone, TV, or tablet for streaming calls, music, and videos.
  • Rechargeable Batteries: No need to change small batteries. Just place them in a charger overnight.
  • App Help: Use the Remote Plus App to adjust volume and settings. Your hearing provider can also fine-tune them remotely.

Unitron Moxi Vivante (V)

The Moxi Vivante is designed to make speech clearer, even in noisy places. It works well for people with mild to severe hearing loss.

  • Bluetooth: Easily connects to phones, TVs, and other devices.
  • App Help: Adjust settings using the Remote Plus App.
  • Tinnitus Relief: Gentle sounds help reduce ringing in your ears.
  • Rechargeable Battery: Provides all-day power with a single charge.

Unitron Stride Blu (B)

The Stride Blu is a behind-the-ear (BTE) hearing aid that works well for mild to severe hearing loss.

  • Bluetooth: Stream calls, music, and TV sound directly to your hearing aids.
  • App Help: Fine-tune settings with the Remote Plus App.
  • Tinnitus Relief: Helps reduce ringing in the ears with relaxing sounds.
  • Rechargeable Battery: No need for disposable batteries. Just charge overnight for all-day use.

Unitron Stride Blu (B) UP

The Stride B UP is built for severe to profound hearing loss. It provides strong, clear sound and is worn behind the ear.

  • Bluetooth: Connects to phones, tablets, and TVs.
  • App Help: Adjust volume and settings using the Remote Plus App.
  • Tinnitus Relief: Soothing sounds help reduce ear ringing.
  • Disposable Batteries: Uses size 675 batteries for long-lasting power.

Unitron Insera Blu (B)

The Insera Blu is a custom-made hearing aid that fits inside the ear for a secure and comfortable feel. It works well for mild to severe hearing loss and comes in different styles:

  • Completely-in-the-Canal
  • Invisible-in-the-Canal
  • In-the-Canal

 

Key features include:

  • Bluetooth: Stream audio from your smartphone, TV, and computers.
  • App Help: Conveniently adjust volume and settings with the Remote Plus App.
  • Tinnitus Relief: Soft background sounds help reduce ringing in the ears.

Assistive Listening Devices & Technology by Unitron

Unitron also offers extra devices to improve your hearing experience:

  • Unitron Partner Mic – Helps you hear conversations better in noisy places.
  • Remote Control – Adjust your hearing aids easily with a small remote.
  • TV Connector – Streams TV audio directly into your hearing aids.
  • IIC Remote – A remote control for Invisible-in-the-Canal (IIC) hearing aids.

Older Unitron Hearing Aid Models Supported by Campbell Hearing Solutions

If you have an older Unitron hearing aid, Campbell Hearing Solutions may still offer servicing and repairs. Contact us to find out more.

Older Unitron models we support:

  • Unitron Moxi B-R
  • Unitron Moxi B-RT
  • Unitron Moxi B-312
  • Unitron Moxi Fit
  • Unitron Moxi Move R
  • Unitron Moxi Jump R T
  • Unitron Insera W 312
  • Unitron Insera 312 Omni
  • Unitron Insera 10 Omni
  • Unitron Insera
  • Unitron Stride P
  • Unitron Stride P R
  • Unitron Max SP
  • Unitron Max UP

Frequently Asked Questions About Unitron Hearing Aids

What is the difference between Unitron Blu and Vivante?

Unitron Vivante (V) is the latest version of receiver-in-the-canal (RIC) hearing aids, replacing Unitron Blu (B). It offers:

  • A new, modern design
  • Improved sound quality
  • A better Remote Plus App

 

For custom in-the-ear (ITE) hearing aids, Unitron Blu remains the most up-to-date option.

What are the levels of technology in Unitron?

Unitron hearing aids come in four levels: 3, 5, 7, and 9.

  • Higher levels (like 9) have better sound processing and stronger noise reduction.
  • They also automatically adjust to noisy environments for clearer hearing.

How long does it take to charge a Unitron hearing aid?

When charging your hearing aids:

  • The green lights blink about 5 times.
  • Once the lights stay solid green, your hearing aids are fully charged.
  • Charging takes about 5 to 7 hours.

Find the Right Unitron Hearing Aid for You

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, we help you choose the best Unitron hearing aid for your needs.

We are an authorized provider of Unitron hearing aids in Granger, IN.

Contact us today to learn more!

Make an Appointment

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, our dedicated team of audiologists brings decades of experience in helping people hear and working with the latest hearing technology.

We go beyond a simple hearing test—our comprehensive evaluations assess hearing, cognition, vision, balance, and dexterity, ensuring a holistic approach to your care.

Let us help you get back to doing what you love with confidence.

Walmart Hearing Aids: What You Need to Know Before You Buy

Hearing loss can make it difficult to enjoy conversations, follow your favorite TV shows, and stay connected with loved ones. So, it’s no surprise that many people look for quick and affordable fixes. With over-the-counter (OTC) hearing aids now available at retailers like Walmart, it may seem like an easy option-but it’s important to know what you’re really getting.

Walmart sells both hearing aids and personal sound amplifiers in stores and online. While these devices may provide limited help for individuals with very mild hearing loss, they often fall short of offering the lasting support and personalization that professional hearing care provides.

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, our licensed audiologists take a personalized, comprehensive approach. We evaluate your hearing, talk through your goals, and help you find the best hearing solution for your unique needs and budget.

Before you purchase a hearing aid from Walmart, here are a few things to consider.

Are Walmart Hearing Aids Legitimate?

Many people don’t realize that not all devices sold as “hearing aids” are actually medical-grade. Walmart sells both FDA-registered hearing aids and personal sound amplifiers (PSAPs)—but they’re not the same thing.

Hearing Aids vs. Sound Amplifiers

  • FDA-Registered Hearing Aids are true medical devices meant to treat mild to moderate hearing loss. Some OTC options fall under this category and can be sold without a prescription.
  • PSAPs simply make all sounds louder. They do not adjust to your hearing profile, are not intended to treat hearing loss, and are not regulated as medical devices.

 

How to Tell the Difference:

  • Look for FDA registration on the label
  • Check if the packaging says “hearing aid” or “amplifier”
  • Devices under $200 are often amplifiers
  • See if the device offers programming or fitting options
  •  

 

If you’re unsure whether a Walmart hearing aid is right for you, our Granger, IN audiologists can guide you in the right direction based on a professional hearing evaluation.

Common Walmart Hearing Aid Models

Walmart offers a variety of hearing devices that appeal to customers for their low price points. Here are a few popular ones:

  • HearingAssist Rechargeable OTC Hearing Aids Rechargeable and labeled as FDA-registered, but lack custom settings and follow-up care.
  • Go Hearing Go Lite & Go Prime Budget-friendly and discreet, but have limited sound control and struggle in noisy settings.
  • Lucid Hearing Engage Includes an app for adjustments, but many users report difficulty setting it up without help.
  • MDHearingAid AIR and VOLT Marketed as “medical-grade,” but sold online with no in-person fitting or local support.

 

While these devices may help some individuals with very mild hearing loss, they often don’t provide the clarity, comfort, or long-term success you get from professionally fit hearing aids.

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, we offer high-quality devices paired with expert guidance and local support-so you get the best experience possible.

Why Walmart Hearing Aids Have Limitations

Though convenient, Walmart’s OTC hearing aids often come with trade-offs:

What’s Missing:

  • No professional hearing test
  • One-size-fits-all design
  • No custom programming
  • Minimal features and support

 

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, we go far beyond the basics with services that include:

  • Comprehensive hearing evaluations
  • Personalized hearing aid fitting and programming
  • Ongoing adjustments, cleanings, and repairs
    Education and support for everyday use

 

Working with a licensed audiologist in Granger, IN ensures your device truly fits your needs—now and in the future.

Do Cheap Hearing Aids Work?

Sometimes—but only for very mild hearing loss. From an audiologist’s perspective, low-cost devices often don’t provide the quality or reliability people expect.

Common issues with cheap hearing aids:

  • Sounds too loud in quiet settings
  • Not enough clarity in noisy places
  • Whistling or feedback
  • Poor fit and comfort
  • No support if something goes wrong

 

You don’t need to spend a fortune to hear better, but you do need the right device—and the right professional by your side. Our audiologists in Granger, IN can help you find an affordable, effective hearing solution tailored to your life.

Do Higher-End Hearing Aids Perform Better?

In most cases, yes. Premium hearing aids offer features that cheaper options can’t match, such as:

  • Advanced noise reduction
  • Bluetooth connectivity and streaming
  • Tinnitus management
  • Rechargeable batteries
    Custom settings for different environments

 

But it’s not just about the features—it’s about the care that comes with them. When you choose Campbell Hearing Solutions, you also get:

  • A professional hearing evaluation
  • A precise fitting by licensed audiologists
  • Follow-up visits and adjustments
  • Warranty coverage and repairs
  • Compassionate, local care

 

That’s the kind of experience you won’t find in a retail store aisle.

Let’s Find the Right Hearing Solution—Together

If you’re considering hearing aids, skip the guesswork. 

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, our audiologists in Granger, IN, are here to help you hear clearly and confidently. 

Call today to schedule your hearing evaluation and start your journey toward better hearing.

Make an Appointment

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, our dedicated team of audiologists brings decades of experience in helping people hear and working with the latest hearing technology.

We go beyond a simple hearing test—our comprehensive evaluations assess hearing, cognition, vision, balance, and dexterity, ensuring a holistic approach to your care.

Let us help you get back to doing what you love with confidence.

Widex Hearing Aids

Campbell Hearing Solutions is an authorized provider of Widex hearing aids in Granger, IN.

Widex is a leading brand in hearing technology, known for bringing artificial intelligence (AI) into hearing aids. Widex hearing aids offer a unique blend of advanced AI features and human-centered design to deliver clear, natural sound.

They’re also an excellent choice for people with tinnitus (ringing in the ears), providing sound therapy to help reduce discomfort and improve daily life.

Current Widex Hearing Aid Models

Widex Allure

The Widex Allure carries the brand’s most advanced hearing aid platform, offering the perfect blend of cutting-edge technology and elegant design. It is crafted to deliver the most natural hearing experience possible, helping you reconnect with the world through clear speech, accurate environmental sound processing, and intuitive adjustments.

Unique Perks and Features:

  • Powered by the W1 Chip: Widex’s fastest chip delivers quick, clear, and natural sound.
  • Allure PureSound™ with ZeroDelay Technology: Helps you hear speech without distortion or echo, creating a more lifelike experience.
  • Speech Enhancer Pro: Lowers background noise so voices stand out, making conversations easier—even in noisy places.
  • Dynamic Feedback Controller: Adapts to your ear’s shape to reduce annoying whistling or squealing sounds.
  • Smooth Streaming: Enjoy music, podcasts, and calls with steady sound—no sudden volume changes or outside interference.
  • Widex Allure App: Easily adjust settings and personalize your hearing experience right from your smartphone.

Widex SmartRIC

The Widex SmartRIC is designed to improve hearing in noisy environments. Its L-shaped design sits higher on the ear, helping make speech clearer. The battery lasts up to 37 hours on a full charge, giving you reliable power throughout the day.

The compact, portable charger holds a week’s worth of power, so you can stay connected wherever you go. It also includes an enhanced microphone cover that reduces background noise for clearer conversations.

Key Features:

  • App: Works with the Widex Moment app to customize your hearing
  • Bluetooth: Connects to both iPhone and Android devices for easy streaming
  • Tinnitus Relief: Includes Zen sound therapy to help manage tinnitus

Widex Moment Sheer RIC

The Widex Moment Sheer RIC (receiver-in-the-canal) combines a sleek, modern design with an upgraded charger. Using dual AI technology, it delivers a personalized sound experience.

Ideal for mild to severe hearing loss, this model is available in both rechargeable and disposable battery versions. The battery lasts around 29 hours without streaming or 16 hours with 8 hours of streaming.

Key Features:

  • Bluetooth: Works with iPhone and Android devices
  • App: Adjust settings through the Moment app
  • Tinnitus Relief: Zen programs provide calming sounds for tinnitus relief

Widex Moment RIC

The Widex Moment RIC is a compact hearing aid that delivers natural sound and faster sound processing, making it easier to hear clearly-especially in noisy places. Many users report higher satisfaction with this model compared to older hearing aids.

This option supports mild to severe hearing loss and comes in rechargeable or disposable battery styles.

Key Features:

  • Bluetooth: Stream audio from iPhone and Android devices
  • App: Customize your hearing settings easily
  • Tinnitus Relief: Includes Zen sound therapy for tinnitus management

Widex Moment BTE

The Widex Moment BTE (behind-the-ear) uses AI technology to learn your sound preferences. The SoundSense Learn feature adapts to your listening environment for more personalized hearing.

This model works for mild to severe hearing loss and is available in rechargeable or disposable battery options (size 13).

Key Features:

  • Bluetooth: Pairs with iPhones and Android devices
  • App: Make easy adjustments using the Moment app
  • Tinnitus Relief: Soothing Zen sounds to reduce ringing in the ears

Widex Moment CROS

The Widex Moment CROS is specially made for individuals who can hear in only one ear. It transfers sound from the non-hearing ear to the hearing ear, improving sound awareness and speech clarity in noisy settings.

This system supports both CROS (one good ear) and BiCROS (hearing loss in both ears, but worse in one) configurations. It also uses less power than many other CROS devices, making it more efficient.

Widex Moment ITE

The Widex Moment ITE (in-the-ear) hearing aid is custom-made to fit your ear comfortably. It adjusts automatically to give you a clear, natural sound experience.

Perfect for mild to moderate hearing loss, it comes with disposable size 10 or 312 batteries, offering up to 105-150 hours of use depending on the model and listening habits.

Key Features:

  • Custom Fit: Personalized design for a secure and comfortable fit
  • Connectivity: Works with Bluetooth and Widex remote control apps
  • Tinnitus Relief: Includes Zen programs to ease tinnitus symptoms

Widex Assistive Listening Devices

Widex also offers additional tools to enhance your hearing experience:

  • Widex TV Play – Streams TV audio directly to your hearing aids
  • Widex Sound Assist – Functions as a microphone, hands-free tool, and remote control
  • Com-Dex, RC-Dex, TV-Dex – Extra accessories for improved hearing aid control

Older Widex Hearing Aids Supported by Campbell Hearing Solutions

If you have an older Widex hearing aid, Campbell Hearing Solutions may still be able to service or repair it. We support the following models:

  • Widex Evoke
  • Widex Beyond
  • Widex Unique
  • Widex Dream

Please contact us for more information about service options.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is the best Widex hearing aid?

The Widex Moment Sheer 440 is the top-tier model. It offers the most advanced features, including enhanced speech clarity and wind noise reduction.

What are the levels of Widex Moment technology?

Widex Moment hearing aids are available in three levels:

  • Widex Moment 220 – Basic level
  • Widex Moment 330 – Standard level
  • Widex Moment 440 – Premium level

Is Widex Moment good for tinnitus?

Yes! Widex Moment hearing aids come with Zen sound therapy programs to help reduce the impact of tinnitus. These relaxing sounds make the ringing in your ears less noticeable and easier to manage.

Find the Right Widex Hearing Aid for You

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, we help you choose the best Widex hearing aid for your needs. We are an authorized provider of Widex hearing aids in Granger, IN.

Contact us today to learn more!

Make an Appointment

At Campbell Hearing Solutions, our dedicated team of audiologists brings decades of experience in helping people hear and working with the latest hearing technology.

We go beyond a simple hearing test—our comprehensive evaluations assess hearing, cognition, vision, balance, and dexterity, ensuring a holistic approach to your care.

Let us help you get back to doing what you love with confidence.

Book Appointment

Home › Karen Smith

Karen Smith

Audiology Assistant

Karen has been with Campbell Hearing Solutions for over 20 years. She has over 25 years of experience working in front and back medical offices, including audiology and ENT.

Audiology & Hearing Aids

Campbell Hearing Solutions provides expert hearing care to help you stay connected to the sounds that matter most. From comprehensive hearing evaluations to advanced hearing aid solutions, our Granger, IN, audiologists are here to support your hearing health with personalized care.

Campbell Hearing Solutions

Phone

(574) 999‑2853

Address

209 Florence Ave. Granger, IN 46530

Email Us

Use our online contact form to email us.

Clinic Hours

Mon - Thurs: 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM
Friday: 8:00 AM - 2:00 PM

Book Appointment
Call Us Now
Our Solutions

Hearing Aids & Protection in Granger, IN

Hearing Aids

Explore our full selection of modern, discreet hearing aids designed to fit your lifestyle. Our experts will help you choose the best device for natural, clear sound.

learn more
Hearing Aid Batteries and Accessories

Keep your hearing aids powered and performing with our high-quality batteries, chargers, and accessories—everything you need in one convenient place.

learn more
Custom Ear Protection

Protect your hearing with custom-fit earplugs and earmolds—ideal for noise, swimming, musicians, and more. Designed for comfort, durability, and maximum protection.

learn more

Supported Hearing Aid Brands

bodyHtml:
Skip to content

Home › Sheree Richardson, M.S. CCC-A

Sheree Richardson, M.S. CCC-A

Audiologist

Sheree Richardson is an audiologist providing hearing tests and hearing aids in Granger, IN.

Sheree is a northwest Indiana native and a 1985 graduate of Purdue University. She brings a strong clinical background and extensive knowledge of hearing aids to her work, with years of experience in otolaryngology clinics, hospital settings, and as an educational trainer for a leading hearing aid manufacturer.

Known for her warm and light-hearted personality, Sheree helps patients feel at ease while delivering high-quality care tailored to their audiology and hearing aid needs.

Outside the clinic, Sheree enjoys singing, performing in community theater, crafting, and spending time with her husband and their golden retriever.

Audiology & Hearing Aids

Campbell Hearing Solutions provides expert hearing care to help you stay connected to the sounds that matter most. From comprehensive hearing evaluations to advanced hearing aid solutions, our Granger, IN, audiologists are here to support your hearing health with personalized care.

Campbell Hearing Solutions

Phone

(574) 999‑2853

Address

209 Florence Ave. Granger, IN 46530

Email Us

Use our online contact form to email us.

Clinic Hours

Mon - Thurs: 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM
Friday: 8:00 AM - 2:00 PM

Book Appointment
Call Us Now
Our Solutions

Hearing Aids & Protection in Granger, IN

Hearing Aids

Explore our full selection of modern, discreet hearing aids designed to fit your lifestyle. Our experts will help you choose the best device for natural, clear sound.

learn more
Hearing Aid Batteries and Accessories

Keep your hearing aids powered and performing with our high-quality batteries, chargers, and accessories—everything you need in one convenient place.

learn more
Custom Ear Protection

Protect your hearing with custom-fit earplugs and earmolds—ideal for noise, swimming, musicians, and more. Designed for comfort, durability, and maximum protection.

learn more

Supported Hearing Aid Brands

bodyHtml:
Skip to content

Thank You!

Our team will review your request and contact you to as soon as we are able.

In the meantime, if you need further assistance, you can call us at our number or reply to the contact form confirmation email that you should receive in the next 5-10 minutes.

bodyHtml:
Skip to content

Thank you for contacting us

Our team will review your request and contact you to as soon as we are able.

In the meantime, if you need further assistance, you can call us at the numbers below or reply to the contact form confirmation email that you should receive in the next 5-10 minutes.

bodyHtml:
Skip to content

Home › Jodie Harman

Jodie Harman

Front Desk Coordinator

Jodie has been with Campbell Hearing Solutions since 2017. Since high school she has always enjoyed working in the medical field and helping patients. As the Front Desk Coordinator, she helps patients through setting appointments, helping with small hearing aid problems, and performing clean and checks. Jodie has always enjoyed being “that” person to help someone and seeing the smile on their face. She finds it incredibly rewarding to see someone be able to hear again and know they appreciate what they have missed.

Audiology & Hearing Aids

Campbell Hearing Solutions provides expert hearing care to help you stay connected to the sounds that matter most. From comprehensive hearing evaluations to advanced hearing aid solutions, our Granger, IN, audiologists are here to support your hearing health with personalized care.

Campbell Hearing Solutions

Phone

(574) 999‑2853

Address

209 Florence Ave. Granger, IN 46530

Email Us

Use our online contact form to email us.

Clinic Hours

bodyHtml:
Skip to content

Home › Stacey Linville

Stacey Linville

Practice Manager

Stacey has been with our team for nearly a decade. She has a bachelor’s degree in organizational management as well as impressive experience in customer service, HR, payroll, and onboarding. Before joining Campbell Hearing Solutions, Stacey spent many years working as a Clinical Coordinator in a large Orthopedic clinic; she also has over 20 years of supervisory experience working in a hospital laboratory.

Stacey has worked in the medical field since she was in high school. She finds this field to be very rewarding as she loves to coach and mentor staff, as well as help patients improve their health.

Outside of work, Stacey enjoys spending time with her family and friends, crafting, fishing at her lake house, and watching her grandkids’ sports.

Audiology & Hearing Aids

Campbell Hearing Solutions provides expert hearing care to help you stay connected to the sounds that matter most. From comprehensive hearing evaluations to advanced hearing aid solutions, our Granger, IN, audiologists are here to support your hearing health with personalized care.

Campbell Hearing Solutions

209 Florence Ave. Granger, IN 46530
